社殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=280|coord={{coord|35|3|7.4|N|135|44|52.7|E}}}} |所在地 = {{jalink|京都府|Q120730}}{{jalink|京都市|Q34600}}{{jalink|北区 (京都市)|Q1154970|北区}}紫竹下竹殿町47 |位置 = {{Coord|35|3|7.4|N|135|44|52.7|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=久我神社}} |祭神 = {{jalink|賀茂建角身命|Q11634939}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（小）
{{jalink|賀茂別雷神社|Q700448}}境外摂社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = 一間社{{jalink|流造|Q210684}}{{jalink|檜皮葺|Q90373477}} |別名 = 氏神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|4月1日|Q2510}}・{{jalink|11月1日|Q2997}}{{Sfn|上賀茂神社（学生社）|2003|pp=51-52}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto city|label=久我神社|position=bottom|width=180|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} [[File:Kuga-jinja (Kyoto) nishi-torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|西鳥居]] '''久我神社'''（くがじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|京都府|Q120730}}{{jalink|京都市|Q34600}}{{jalink|北区_(京都市)|Q1154970|北区}}紫竹下竹殿町にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}で、現在は{{jalink|賀茂別雷神社|Q700448}}（上賀茂神社）の境外{{jalink|摂社|Q135009904}}（第八摂社）。 旧称は「'''氏神社'''」。上賀茂神社の西約1キロメートルの地に鎮座する{{Sfn|上賀茂神社（学生社）|2003|pp=51-52}}。 === 祭神 === 祭神は次の1柱{{Sfn|上賀茂神社（学生社）|2003|pp=51-52}}。 * '''{{jalink|賀茂建角身命|Q11634939}}''' （かもたけつぬみのみこと） *: {{jalink|賀茂県主氏|Q2975287|賀茂県主}}（賀茂氏）の祖神。上賀茂神社祭神の{{jalink|賀茂別雷命|Q11634943}}（かもわけいかづちのみこと）の祖父にあたり、{{jalink|神武天皇|Q200188}}の{{jalink|神武東征|Q11590444|東征}}で先導した{{jalink|八咫烏|Q745463}}（やたがらす）と同一視される。 古くより社名を「氏神社」を称することから、祭神は賀茂氏祖先神の賀茂建角身命とされるが、上賀茂神社文書によれば{{jalink|近世|Q5308718}}には{{jalink|国之常立神|Q478504|国常立尊}}等の異説も存在した{{Sfn|久我神社（式内社）|1979}}。 === 歴史 === {{座標一覧}} === 創建 === [[File:Koga-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|久我神社（京都市{{jalink|伏見区|Q1197771}}）}}]] 創建は不詳。当社は、{{jalink|賀茂県主氏|Q2975287}}が{{jalink|大和国|Q907495|大和}}から{{jalink|山城国|Q749276|山城}}に移住するに際して祀った社の1つと見られている{{Sfn|久我神社（神々）|1985}}。『{{jalink|風土記|Q1194534|山城国風土記}}』逸文『釈日本紀』巻9所引『山城国風土記』逸文。によると、賀茂建角身命は神武天皇の東征に功を成したのち、大和から「山代の国の岡田の賀茂」、「葛野川（{{jalink|桂川 (淀川水系) |Q2909307|桂川}}）と賀茂河（{{jalink|鴨川 (京都府)|Q27218|鴨川}}）との会う所」、「久我の国の北の山基」の順に遷座したといい、それぞれの地には現在も賀茂氏の氏神が祀られている{{Sfn|久我神社（神々）|1985}}。それぞれの比定地は次の通り。 # 山代の国の岡田の賀茂 - 現在の京都府{{jalink|木津川市|Q990352}}加茂町。{{jalink|式内社}}の{{jalink|岡田鴨神社|Q11473738}}（{{Coord|34|45|41.55|N|135|52|34.92|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|name=1. 岡田鴨神社}}）が鎮座。 # 葛野川と賀茂河との会う所 - 現在の京都府京都市{{jalink|伏見区|Q1197771}}。式内社の久何神社（現・{{jalink|久我神社 (京都市伏見区)||久我神社}}、{{Coord|34|56|26.37|N|135|43|48.94|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|name=2. 久何神社（現・久我神社）}}）が鎮座。 # 久我の国の北の山基 - 当社付近。 「{{jalink|山背久我国造||久我（国）}}」の地名については、当社一帯の古称であるが賀茂氏勢力の広がりにつれて衰えたとする説がある一方、本来は久何神社（久我神社）のある{{jalink|乙訓郡|Q376858}}の呼称で当地に踏襲されたとする説や、乙訓郡ではなく南山城の{{jalink|紀伊郡|Q11604966}}を本来の呼称と見る説が挙げられている{{Sfn|久我神社（平凡社）|1979}}{{Sfn|久我神社（神々）|1985}}。 当社が現在地に祀られたことに関しては、付近に賀茂建角身命の墳墓があったためとする伝承があるが、明らかではない{{Sfn|上賀茂神社（学生社）|2003|pp=51-52}}。 === 概史 === {{jalink|六国史|Q1151168|国史}}では、{{jalink|貞観 (日本)|Q1075635|貞観}}元年（{{jalink|859年|Q23897}}）『日本三代実録』貞観元年（859年）正月27日条。に「久我神」の{{jalink|神階|Q11591025}}が正六位上から従五位下へ昇叙されたと見える{{Sfn|久我神社（神々）|1985}}。また、{{jalink|延長 (元号)|Q1327274|延長}}5年（{{jalink|927年|Q30470}}）成立の『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}では{{jalink|山城国|Q749276}}{{jalink|愛宕郡|Q10981345}}に「久我神社」として{{jalink|式内社}}の記載が見え、これらの社は当社に比定される{{Sfn|久我神社（神々）|1985}}。 当社は賀茂社の『嘉元年中行事』において末社としての記載が見えることから、{{jalink|平安時代|Q193292}}後期頃から賀茂社の末社に列したと推測されている{{Sfn|久我神社（平凡社）|1979}}。また、社名も{{jalink|鎌倉時代|Q236205}}以降は「氏神社」と称していた境内説明板。。ただし祭祀のうえでは、{{jalink|中世|Q12554}}にはすでに賀茂氏の氏神としての性格は薄れ、郷民の社としての性格を強めている{{Sfn|久我神社（式内社）|1979}}。 {{jalink|宝暦|Q1194397}}4年（{{jalink|1754年|Q7623}}）の『{{jalink|山城名跡巡行志}}』では「紫竹の北端には大宮の森、一名を栢の森と呼ぶ。古社があり、{{jalink|鳥居|Q234731}}は西向きで拝殿は南向き。」という記述が見える。 {{jalink|近世|Q5308718}}以降、当社は主に「氏神社」や「大宮」と称されていたが、{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}）に社名を「久我神社」に改めて現在に至っている{{Sfn|久我神社（神々）|1985}}。 === 神階 === * {{jalink|貞観 (日本)|Q1075635|貞観}}元年（{{jalink|859年|Q23897}}）1月27日、正六位上から従五位下 （『{{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981}}』） - 表記は「久我神」。 === 境内 === [[File:Kuga-jinja2.JPG|thumb|200px|ケヤキの切り株
{{Smaller|樹齢は600年といわれる{{Sfn|上賀茂神社（学生社）|2003|pp=51-52}}。}}]] {{jalink|貞享|Q1146221}}3年（{{jalink|1686年|Q7664}}）の『{{jalink|雍州府志|Q18460389}}』では境内は「大宮の森」と俗称されるが、敷地内には現在もその面影を伝える{{jalink|ケヤキ|Q920344}}の巨木を残している。由緒のうえでも賀茂氏の移住伝承を現在に伝えており、京都の歴史を明らかにする環境を良好に残しているとして、境内は京都市指定史跡に指定されている。 現在の本殿・拝殿は{{jalink|寛永|Q1192913}}5年（{{jalink|1628年|Q6840}}）の造営{{Sfn|上賀茂神社（学生社）|2003|pp=51-52}}。本殿は一間社{{jalink|流造|Q210684}}の形式で、{{jalink|檜皮葺|Q90373477}}の屋根であり、上賀茂神社の他摂社と同じ造りになる。また拝殿は梁間一間・桁行二間の{{jalink|切妻造|Q13308575}}妻入で、屋根は同じく檜皮葺である。 なお、創建当時より久我神社は「大宮」と称されたとして、一帯の地域名が「大宮」（大宮郷）、門前の通り名は「大宮通」と定められたとする伝えがある。その通りとは{{jalink|大徳寺通|Q11435442}}で、地元では今日でも「旧大宮通」と称されている。 === 祭事 === * 春祭 （{{jalink|4月1日|Q2510}}） * {{jalink|八朔祭}} （{{jalink|9月1日|Q2859}}） * 秋祭 （{{jalink|11月1日|Q2997}}） * 神幸祭 （{{jalink|11月3日|Q2995}}） - 旧大宮郷四学区（大宮・待鳳・紫竹・元町）の氏子町内を{{jalink|神輿|Q979940}}が巡行する。 春秋の例祭では「牽馬の儀（ひきうまのぎ）」として、上賀茂神社の神馬が本殿を3周する神事が行われる。 === 文化財 === === 京都市指定史跡 === * 久我神社境内 - 昭和62年5月1日指定[https://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/bunshi/page/0000005881.html 京都市指定・登録文化財－史跡－北区]（京都市ホームページ「京都市情報館」）。。 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ;原典 {{Reflist|group="原"}} === 参考文献 === * 神社由緒書 * 境内説明板（京都市設置）　 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=久我神社|title={{jalink|日本歴史地名大系|Q17213509}} 27 京都市の地名|publisher={{jalink|平凡社|Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harvid|久我神社（平凡社）|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=式内社研究会編|author=泉谷康夫|chapter=久我神社|year=1979|title=式内社調査報告 第1巻|publisher={{jalink|皇學館大学|Q1797584}}出版部|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|久我神社（式内社）|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor={{jalink|谷川健一|Q2392596}}編|author=源城政好|year=1986|chapter=久我神社|title=日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 5 山城・近江|publisher={{jalink|白水社|Q4346470}}|isbn=4560022151|ref={{Harvid|久我神社（神々）|1985}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=建内光儀|chapter=摂末社の由緒|year=2003|title=上賀茂神社|publisher={{jalink|学生社|Q11448819}}|isbn=4311407203|ref={{Harvid|上賀茂神社（学生社）|2003}}}} * 『京都の地名検証』（京都地名研究会編、勉誠出版、2005年） * 『探訪 京都・上賀茂と二つの鞍馬街道－その今昔』（西村勁一郎(個人)、2008年） === 関連項目 === {{Commonscat|Kuga-jinja}} * {{jalink|賀茂別雷神社|Q700448}}（上賀茂神社） * {{jalink|大宮通|Q11434468}}（新大宮通） * {{jalink|航空神社|Q30319396}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くかしんしや きようとしきたく}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuga Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kuga Shrine]][[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:京都市の神社]] [[Category:京都市北区の歴史]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:賀茂県主氏|社くかしんしや きようとしきたく]] [[Category:京都市北区の建築物]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

社殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=280|coord={{coord|35|3|7.4|N|135|44|52.7|E}}}} |所在地 = {{jalink|京都府|Q120730}}{{jalink|京都市|Q34600}}{{jalink|北区 (京都市)|Q1154970|北区}}紫竹下竹殿町47 |位置 = {{Coord|35|3|7.4|N|135|44|52.7|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=久我神社}} |祭神 = {{jalink|賀茂建角身命|Q11634939}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（小）
{{jalink|賀茂別雷神社|Q700448}}境外摂社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = 一間社{{jalink|流造|Q210684}}{{jalink|檜皮葺|Q90373477}} |別名 = 氏神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|4月1日|Q2510}}・{{jalink|11月1日|Q2997}}{{Sfn|上賀茂神社（学生社）|2003|pp=51-52}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto city|label=久我神社|position=bottom|width=180|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} [[File:Kuga-jinja (Kyoto) nishi-torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|西鳥居]] '''久我神社'''（くがじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|京都府|Q120730}}{{jalink|京都市|Q34600}}{{jalink|北区_(京都市)|Q1154970|北区}}紫竹下竹殿町にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}で、現在は{{jalink|賀茂別雷神社|Q700448}}（上賀茂神社）の境外{{jalink|摂社|Q135009904}}（第八摂社）。 旧称は「'''氏神社'''」。上賀茂神社の西約1キロメートルの地に鎮座する{{Sfn|上賀茂神社（学生社）|2003|pp=51-52}}。 === 祭神 === 祭神は次の1柱{{Sfn|上賀茂神社（学生社）|2003|pp=51-52}}。 * '''{{jalink|賀茂建角身命|Q11634939}}''' （かもたけつぬみのみこと） *: {{jalink|賀茂県主氏|Q2975287|賀茂県主}}（賀茂氏）の祖神。上賀茂神社祭神の{{jalink|賀茂別雷命|Q11634943}}（かもわけいかづちのみこと）の祖父にあたり、{{jalink|神武天皇|Q200188}}の{{jalink|神武東征|Q11590444|東征}}で先導した{{jalink|八咫烏|Q745463}}（やたがらす）と同一視される。 古くより社名を「氏神社」を称することから、祭神は賀茂氏祖先神の賀茂建角身命とされるが、上賀茂神社文書によれば{{jalink|近世|Q5308718}}には{{jalink|国之常立神|Q478504|国常立尊}}等の異説も存在した{{Sfn|久我神社（式内社）|1979}}。 === 歴史 === {{座標一覧}} === 創建 === [[File:Koga-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|久我神社（京都市{{jalink|伏見区|Q1197771}}）}}]] 創建は不詳。当社は、{{jalink|賀茂県主氏|Q2975287}}が{{jalink|大和国|Q907495|大和}}から{{jalink|山城国|Q749276|山城}}に移住するに際して祀った社の1つと見られている{{Sfn|久我神社（神々）|1985}}。『{{jalink|風土記|Q1194534|山城国風土記}}』逸文『釈日本紀』巻9所引『山城国風土記』逸文。によると、賀茂建角身命は神武天皇の東征に功を成したのち、大和から「山代の国の岡田の賀茂」、「葛野川（{{jalink|桂川 (淀川水系) |Q2909307|桂川}}）と賀茂河（{{jalink|鴨川 (京都府)|Q27218|鴨川}}）との会う所」、「久我の国の北の山基」の順に遷座したといい、それぞれの地には現在も賀茂氏の氏神が祀られている{{Sfn|久我神社（神々）|1985}}。それぞれの比定地は次の通り。 # 山代の国の岡田の賀茂 - 現在の京都府{{jalink|木津川市|Q990352}}加茂町。{{jalink|式内社}}の{{jalink|岡田鴨神社|Q11473738}}（{{Coord|34|45|41.55|N|135|52|34.92|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|name=1. 岡田鴨神社}}）が鎮座。 # 葛野川と賀茂河との会う所 - 現在の京都府京都市{{jalink|伏見区|Q1197771}}。式内社の久何神社（現・{{jalink|久我神社 (京都市伏見区)||久我神社}}、{{Coord|34|56|26.37|N|135|43|48.94|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|name=2. 久何神社（現・久我神社）}}）が鎮座。 # 久我の国の北の山基 - 当社付近。 「{{jalink|山背久我国造||久我（国）}}」の地名については、当社一帯の古称であるが賀茂氏勢力の広がりにつれて衰えたとする説がある一方、本来は久何神社（久我神社）のある{{jalink|乙訓郡|Q376858}}の呼称で当地に踏襲されたとする説や、乙訓郡ではなく南山城の{{jalink|紀伊郡|Q11604966}}を本来の呼称と見る説が挙げられている{{Sfn|久我神社（平凡社）|1979}}{{Sfn|久我神社（神々）|1985}}。 当社が現在地に祀られたことに関しては、付近に賀茂建角身命の墳墓があったためとする伝承があるが、明らかではない{{Sfn|上賀茂神社（学生社）|2003|pp=51-52}}。 === 概史 === {{jalink|六国史|Q1151168|国史}}では、{{jalink|貞観 (日本)|Q1075635|貞観}}元年（{{jalink|859年|Q23897}}）『日本三代実録』貞観元年（859年）正月27日条。に「久我神」の{{jalink|神階|Q11591025}}が正六位上から従五位下へ昇叙されたと見える{{Sfn|久我神社（神々）|1985}}。また、{{jalink|延長 (元号)|Q1327274|延長}}5年（{{jalink|927年|Q30470}}）成立の『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}では{{jalink|山城国|Q749276}}{{jalink|愛宕郡|Q10981345}}に「久我神社」として{{jalink|式内社}}の記載が見え、これらの社は当社に比定される{{Sfn|久我神社（神々）|1985}}。 当社は賀茂社の『嘉元年中行事』において末社としての記載が見えることから、{{jalink|平安時代|Q193292}}後期頃から賀茂社の末社に列したと推測されている{{Sfn|久我神社（平凡社）|1979}}。また、社名も{{jalink|鎌倉時代|Q236205}}以降は「氏神社」と称していた境内説明板。。ただし祭祀のうえでは、{{jalink|中世|Q12554}}にはすでに賀茂氏の氏神としての性格は薄れ、郷民の社としての性格を強めている{{Sfn|久我神社（式内社）|1979}}。 {{jalink|宝暦|Q1194397}}4年（{{jalink|1754年|Q7623}}）の『{{jalink|山城名跡巡行志}}』では「紫竹の北端には大宮の森、一名を栢の森と呼ぶ。古社があり、{{jalink|鳥居|Q234731}}は西向きで拝殿は南向き。」という記述が見える。 {{jalink|近世|Q5308718}}以降、当社は主に「氏神社」や「大宮」と称されていたが、{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}）に社名を「久我神社」に改めて現在に至っている{{Sfn|久我神社（神々）|1985}}。 === 神階 === * {{jalink|貞観 (日本)|Q1075635|貞観}}元年（{{jalink|859年|Q23897}}）1月27日、正六位上から従五位下 （『{{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981}}』） - 表記は「久我神」。 === 境内 === [[File:Kuga-jinja2.JPG|thumb|200px|ケヤキの切り株
{{Smaller|樹齢は600年といわれる{{Sfn|上賀茂神社（学生社）|2003|pp=51-52}}。}}]] {{jalink|貞享|Q1146221}}3年（{{jalink|1686年|Q7664}}）の『{{jalink|雍州府志|Q18460389}}』では境内は「大宮の森」と俗称されるが、敷地内には現在もその面影を伝える{{jalink|ケヤキ|Q920344}}の巨木を残している。由緒のうえでも賀茂氏の移住伝承を現在に伝えており、京都の歴史を明らかにする環境を良好に残しているとして、境内は京都市指定史跡に指定されている。 現在の本殿・拝殿は{{jalink|寛永|Q1192913}}5年（{{jalink|1628年|Q6840}}）の造営{{Sfn|上賀茂神社（学生社）|2003|pp=51-52}}。本殿は一間社{{jalink|流造|Q210684}}の形式で、{{jalink|檜皮葺|Q90373477}}の屋根であり、上賀茂神社の他摂社と同じ造りになる。また拝殿は梁間一間・桁行二間の{{jalink|切妻造|Q13308575}}妻入で、屋根は同じく檜皮葺である。 なお、創建当時より久我神社は「大宮」と称されたとして、一帯の地域名が「大宮」（大宮郷）、門前の通り名は「大宮通」と定められたとする伝えがある。その通りとは{{jalink|大徳寺通|Q11435442}}で、地元では今日でも「旧大宮通」と称されている。 === 祭事 === * 春祭 （{{jalink|4月1日|Q2510}}） * {{jalink|八朔祭}} （{{jalink|9月1日|Q2859}}） * 秋祭 （{{jalink|11月1日|Q2997}}） * 神幸祭 （{{jalink|11月3日|Q2995}}） - 旧大宮郷四学区（大宮・待鳳・紫竹・元町）の氏子町内を{{jalink|神輿|Q979940}}が巡行する。 春秋の例祭では「牽馬の儀（ひきうまのぎ）」として、上賀茂神社の神馬が本殿を3周する神事が行われる。 === 文化財 === === 京都市指定史跡 === * 久我神社境内 - 昭和62年5月1日指定[https://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/bunshi/page/0000005881.html 京都市指定・登録文化財－史跡－北区]（京都市ホームページ「京都市情報館」）。。 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ;原典 {{Reflist|group="原"}} === 参考文献 === * 神社由緒書 * 境内説明板（京都市設置）　 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=久我神社|title={{jalink|日本歴史地名大系|Q17213509}} 27 京都市の地名|publisher={{jalink|平凡社|Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harvid|久我神社（平凡社）|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=式内社研究会編|author=泉谷康夫|chapter=久我神社|year=1979|title=式内社調査報告 第1巻|publisher={{jalink|皇學館大学|Q1797584}}出版部|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|久我神社（式内社）|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor={{jalink|谷川健一|Q2392596}}編|author=源城政好|year=1986|chapter=久我神社|title=日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 5 山城・近江|publisher={{jalink|白水社|Q4346470}}|isbn=4560022151|ref={{Harvid|久我神社（神々）|1985}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=建内光儀|chapter=摂末社の由緒|year=2003|title=上賀茂神社|publisher={{jalink|学生社|Q11448819}}|isbn=4311407203|ref={{Harvid|上賀茂神社（学生社）|2003}}}} * 『京都の地名検証』（京都地名研究会編、勉誠出版、2005年） * 『探訪 京都・上賀茂と二つの鞍馬街道－その今昔』（西村勁一郎(個人)、2008年） === 関連項目 === {{Commonscat|Kuga-jinja}} * {{jalink|賀茂別雷神社|Q700448}}（上賀茂神社） * {{jalink|大宮通|Q11434468}}（新大宮通） * {{jalink|航空神社|Q30319396}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くかしんしや きようとしきたく}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuga Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kuga Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:京都市の神社]] [[Category:京都市北区の歴史]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:賀茂県主氏|社くかしんしや きようとしきたく]] [[Category:京都市北区の建築物]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

category|Category:Kuga-jinja}} [[JA:久我神社 (京都市北区)]] {{wikidata link|Q11369376}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Kuga Shrine |画像 = [[File:Kuga-jinja (Kyoto) shaden.JPG|250px]]
Shrine building
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=280|coord={{coord|35|3|7.4|N|135|44|52.7|E}}}} |所在地 = {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}{{jalink|Kyoto|Q34600}}{{jalink|Kita-ku, Kyoto|Q1154970|Kita Ward}} Shichiku-shimotakeno-donochō 47 |位置 = {{Coord|35|3|7.4|N|135|44|52.7|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kuga Shrine}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Kamo Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto|Q11634939}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor)
Outside-precinct sessha of {{jalink|Kamowakeikazuchi Shrine|Q700448}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = One-bay {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}}, {{jalink|hiwada-buki|Q90373477}} |別名 = Ujigami Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|April 1|Q2510}} / {{jalink|November 1|Q2997}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto city|label=Kuga Shrine|position=bottom|width=180|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} [[File:Kuga-jinja (Kyoto) nishi-torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|West torii]] '''Kuga Shrine''' (くがじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Shichiku-shimotakeno-donochō, Kita Ward, Kyoto, Kyoto Prefecture. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and currently an outside-precinct {{jalink|sessha|Q135009904}} (8th sessha) of {{jalink|Kamowakeikazuchi Shrine|Q700448}} (Kamigamo Shrine). === Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Kamo Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto|Q11634939}} === History === Founding is unknown. It is considered one shrine established when the {{jalink|Kamo Agatanushi clan|Q2975287}} moved from {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}} to {{jalink|Yamashiro Province|Q749276}}. In national histories, in {{jalink|859|Q23897}} (Jōgan 1), divine rank of “Kuga-no-kami” was raised from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower. In the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}' (927), it appears in Atago District, Yamashiro Province as “Kuga Shrine,” and is identified with this shrine. It appears as a subordinate shrine of Kamo Shrine in medieval records, and from around the late Heian period is inferred to have become a Kamo subordinate shrine. From the Kamakura period onward, the name “Ujigami Shrine” was commonly used. In {{jalink|1872|Q7753}} (Meiji 5), the shrine name was restored to “Kuga Shrine,” which remains in use. === Divine ranks === * {{jalink|859|Q23897}} (Jōgan 1), Jan 27: from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower ('{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}') === Precincts === Current main sanctuary and worship hall were built in {{jalink|1628|Q6840}} (Kan'ei 5). Main sanctuary is one-bay nagare-zukuri with hiwada roof, same style as other sessha of Kamigamo Shrine. === Festivals === * Spring festival (April 1) * Hassaku festival (September 1) * Autumn festival (November 1) * Shinkō festival (November 3) === Cultural property === === Kyoto City-designated Historic Site === * Kuga Shrine precincts - designated May 1, 1987. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === {{Commonscat|Kuga-jinja}} * {{jalink|Kamowakeikazuchi Shrine|Q700448}} (Kamigamo Shrine) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くかしんしや きようとしきたく}} [[Category:Weird possibly ai botched translations]] == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuga Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kuga Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:京都市の神社]] [[Category:京都市北区の歴史]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:賀茂県主氏|社くかしんしや きようとしきたく]] [[Category:京都市北区の建築物]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=280|coord={{coord|35|3|7.4|N|135|44|52.7|E}}}} |所在地 = {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}{{jalink|Kyoto|Q34600}}{{jalink|Kita-ku, Kyoto|Q1154970|Kita Ward}} Shichiku-shimotakeno-donochō 47 |位置 = {{Coord|35|3|7.4|N|135|44|52.7|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kuga Shrine}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Kamo Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto|Q11634939}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor)
Outside-precinct sessha of {{jalink|Kamowakeikazuchi Shrine|Q700448}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = One-bay {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}}, {{jalink|hiwada-buki|Q90373477}} |別名 = Ujigami Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|April 1|Q2510}} / {{jalink|November 1|Q2997}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto city|label=Kuga Shrine|position=bottom|width=180|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} [[File:Kuga-jinja (Kyoto) nishi-torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|West torii]] '''Kuga Shrine''' (くがじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Shichiku-shimotakeno-donochō, Kita Ward, Kyoto, Kyoto Prefecture. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and currently an outside-precinct {{jalink|sessha|Q135009904}} (8th sessha) of {{jalink|Kamowakeikazuchi Shrine|Q700448}} (Kamigamo Shrine). === Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Kamo Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto|Q11634939}} === History === Founding is unknown. It is considered one shrine established when the {{jalink|Kamo Agatanushi clan|Q2975287}} moved from {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}} to {{jalink|Yamashiro Province|Q749276}}. In national histories, in {{jalink|859|Q23897}} (Jōgan 1), divine rank of “Kuga-no-kami” was raised from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower. In the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}' (927), it appears in Atago District, Yamashiro Province as “Kuga Shrine,” and is identified with this shrine. It appears as a subordinate shrine of Kamo Shrine in medieval records, and from around the late Heian period is inferred to have become a Kamo subordinate shrine. From the Kamakura period onward, the name “Ujigami Shrine” was commonly used. In {{jalink|1872|Q7753}} (Meiji 5), the shrine name was restored to “Kuga Shrine,” which remains in use. === Divine ranks === * {{jalink|859|Q23897}} (Jōgan 1), Jan 27: from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower ('{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}') === Precincts === Current main sanctuary and worship hall were built in {{jalink|1628|Q6840}} (Kan'ei 5). Main sanctuary is one-bay nagare-zukuri with hiwada roof, same style as other sessha of Kamigamo Shrine. === Festivals === * Spring festival (April 1) * Hassaku festival (September 1) * Autumn festival (November 1) * Shinkō festival (November 3) === Cultural property === === Kyoto City-designated Historic Site === * Kuga Shrine precincts - designated May 1, 1987. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === {{Commonscat|Kuga-jinja}} * {{jalink|Kamowakeikazuchi Shrine|Q700448}} (Kamigamo Shrine) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くかしんしや きようとしきたく}} [[Category:Weird possibly ai botched translations]] == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuga Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kuga Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:京都市の神社]] [[Category:京都市北区の歴史]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:賀茂県主氏|社くかしんしや きようとしきたく]] [[Category:京都市北区の建築物]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=280|coord={{coord|35|3|7.4|N|135|44|52.7|E}}}} |所在地 = {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}{{jalink|Kyoto|Q34600}}{{jalink|Kita-ku, Kyoto|Q1154970|Kita Ward}} Shichiku-shimotakeno-donochō 47 |位置 = {{Coord|35|3|7.4|N|135|44|52.7|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kuga Shrine}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Kamo Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto|Q11634939}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor)
Outside-precinct sessha of {{jalink|Kamowakeikazuchi Shrine|Q700448}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = One-bay {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}}, {{jalink|hiwada-buki|Q90373477}} |別名 = Ujigami Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|April 1|Q2510}} / {{jalink|November 1|Q2997}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto city|label=Kuga Shrine|position=bottom|width=180|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} [[File:Kuga-jinja (Kyoto) nishi-torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|West torii]] '''Kuga Shrine''' (くがじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Shichiku-shimotakeno-donochō, Kita Ward, Kyoto, Kyoto Prefecture. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and currently an outside-precinct {{jalink|sessha|Q135009904}} (8th sessha) of {{jalink|Kamowakeikazuchi Shrine|Q700448}} (Kamigamo Shrine). === Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Kamo Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto|Q11634939}} === History === Founding is unknown. It is considered one shrine established when the {{jalink|Kamo Agatanushi clan|Q2975287}} moved from {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}} to {{jalink|Yamashiro Province|Q749276}}. In national histories, in {{jalink|859|Q23897}} (Jōgan 1), divine rank of “Kuga-no-kami” was raised from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower. In the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}' (927), it appears in Atago District, Yamashiro Province as “Kuga Shrine,” and is identified with this shrine. It appears as a subordinate shrine of Kamo Shrine in medieval records, and from around the late Heian period is inferred to have become a Kamo subordinate shrine. From the Kamakura period onward, the name “Ujigami Shrine” was commonly used. In {{jalink|1872|Q7753}} (Meiji 5), the shrine name was restored to “Kuga Shrine,” which remains in use. === Divine ranks === * {{jalink|859|Q23897}} (Jōgan 1), Jan 27: from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower ('{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}') === Precincts === Current main sanctuary and worship hall were built in {{jalink|1628|Q6840}} (Kan'ei 5). Main sanctuary is one-bay nagare-zukuri with hiwada roof, same style as other sessha of Kamigamo Shrine. === Festivals === * Spring festival (April 1) * Hassaku festival (September 1) * Autumn festival (November 1) * Shinkō festival (November 3) === Cultural property === === Kyoto City-designated Historic Site === * Kuga Shrine precincts - designated May 1, 1987. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === {{Commonscat|Kuga-jinja}} * {{jalink|Kamowakeikazuchi Shrine|Q700448}} (Kamigamo Shrine) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くかしんしや きようとしきたく}} [[Category:Weird possibly ai botched translations]] == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuga Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kuga Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:京都市の神社]] [[Category:京都市北区の歴史]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:賀茂県主氏|社くかしんしや きようとしきたく]] [[Category:京都市北区の建築物]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

Outside-precinct sessha of {{jalink|Kamowakeikazuchi Shrine|Q700448}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = One-bay {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}}, {{jalink|hiwada-buki|Q90373477}} |別名 = Ujigami Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|April 1|Q2510}} / {{jalink|November 1|Q2997}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto city|label=Kuga Shrine|position=bottom|width=180|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} [[File:Kuga-jinja (Kyoto) nishi-torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|West torii]] '''Kuga Shrine''' (くがじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Shichiku-shimotakeno-donochō, Kita Ward, Kyoto, Kyoto Prefecture. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and currently an outside-precinct {{jalink|sessha|Q135009904}} (8th sessha) of {{jalink|Kamowakeikazuchi Shrine|Q700448}} (Kamigamo Shrine). === Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Kamo Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto|Q11634939}} === History === Founding is unknown. It is considered one shrine established when the {{jalink|Kamo Agatanushi clan|Q2975287}} moved from {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}} to {{jalink|Yamashiro Province|Q749276}}. In national histories, in {{jalink|859|Q23897}} (Jōgan 1), divine rank of “Kuga-no-kami” was raised from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower. In the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}' (927), it appears in Atago District, Yamashiro Province as “Kuga Shrine,” and is identified with this shrine. It appears as a subordinate shrine of Kamo Shrine in medieval records, and from around the late Heian period is inferred to have become a Kamo subordinate shrine. From the Kamakura period onward, the name “Ujigami Shrine” was commonly used. In {{jalink|1872|Q7753}} (Meiji 5), the shrine name was restored to “Kuga Shrine,” which remains in use. === Divine ranks === * {{jalink|859|Q23897}} (Jōgan 1), Jan 27: from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower ('{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}') === Precincts === Current main sanctuary and worship hall were built in {{jalink|1628|Q6840}} (Kan'ei 5). Main sanctuary is one-bay nagare-zukuri with hiwada roof, same style as other sessha of Kamigamo Shrine. === Festivals === * Spring festival (April 1) * Hassaku festival (September 1) * Autumn festival (November 1) * Shinkō festival (November 3) === Cultural property === === Kyoto City-designated Historic Site === * Kuga Shrine precincts - designated May 1, 1987. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === {{Commonscat|Kuga-jinja}} * {{jalink|Kamowakeikazuchi Shrine|Q700448}} (Kamigamo Shrine) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くかしんしや きようとしきたく}} [[Category:Weird possibly ai botched translations]] == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuga Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kuga Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:京都市の神社]] [[Category:京都市北区の歴史]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:賀茂県主氏|社くかしんしや きようとしきたく]] [[Category:京都市北区の建築物]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

category|Category:Kuga-jinja}} [[JA:久我神社 (京都市北区)]] {{wikidata link|Q11369376}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Kuga Shrine |画像 = [[File:Kuga-jinja (Kyoto) shaden.JPG|250px]]
Shrine building
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=280|coord={{coord|35|3|7.4|N|135|44|52.7|E}}}} |所在地 = {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}{{jalink|Kyoto|Q34600}}{{jalink|Kita-ku, Kyoto|Q1154970|Kita Ward}} Shichiku-shimotakeno-donochō 47 |位置 = {{Coord|35|3|7.4|N|135|44|52.7|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kuga Shrine}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Kamo Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto|Q11634939}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor)
Outside-precinct sessha of {{jalink|Kamowakeikazuchi Shrine|Q700448}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = One-bay {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}}, {{jalink|hiwada-buki|Q90373477}} |別名 = Ujigami Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|April 1|Q2510}} / {{jalink|November 1|Q2997}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto city|label=Kuga Shrine|position=bottom|width=180|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} [[File:Kuga-jinja (Kyoto) nishi-torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|West torii]] '''Kuga Shrine''' (くがじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Shichiku-shimotakeno-donochō, Kita Ward, Kyoto, Kyoto Prefecture. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and currently an outside-precinct {{jalink|sessha|Q135009904}} (8th sessha) of {{jalink|Kamowakeikazuchi Shrine|Q700448}} (Kamigamo Shrine). === Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Kamo Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto|Q11634939}} === History === Founding is unknown. It is considered one shrine established when the {{jalink|Kamo Agatanushi clan|Q2975287}} moved from {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}} to {{jalink|Yamashiro Province|Q749276}}. In national histories, in {{jalink|859|Q23897}} (Jōgan 1), divine rank of “Kuga-no-kami” was raised from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower. In the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}' (927), it appears in Atago District, Yamashiro Province as “Kuga Shrine,” and is identified with this shrine. It appears as a subordinate shrine of Kamo Shrine in medieval records, and from around the late Heian period is inferred to have become a Kamo subordinate shrine. From the Kamakura period onward, the name “Ujigami Shrine” was commonly used. In {{jalink|1872|Q7753}} (Meiji 5), the shrine name was restored to “Kuga Shrine,” which remains in use. === Divine ranks === * {{jalink|859|Q23897}} (Jōgan 1), Jan 27: from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower ('{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}') === Precincts === Current main sanctuary and worship hall were built in {{jalink|1628|Q6840}} (Kan'ei 5). Main sanctuary is one-bay nagare-zukuri with hiwada roof, same style as other sessha of Kamigamo Shrine. === Festivals === * Spring festival (April 1) * Hassaku festival (September 1) * Autumn festival (November 1) * Shinkō festival (November 3) === Cultural property === === Kyoto City-designated Historic Site === * Kuga Shrine precincts - designated May 1, 1987. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === {{Commonscat|Kuga-jinja}} * {{jalink|Kamowakeikazuchi Shrine|Q700448}} (Kamigamo Shrine) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くかしんしや きようとしきたく}} [[Category:Weird possibly ai botched translations]] == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuga Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kuga Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:京都市の神社]] [[Category:京都市北区の歴史]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:賀茂県主氏|社くかしんしや きようとしきたく]] [[Category:京都市北区の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

category|Category:Kuga-jinja}} [[JA:久我神社 (京都市北区)]] {{wikidata link|Q11369376}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 久我神社 |画像 = [[File:Kuga-jinja (Kyoto) shaden.JPG|250px]]
社殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=280|coord={{coord|35|3|7.4|N|135|44|52.7|E}}}} |所在地 = {{jalink|京都府|Q120730}}{{jalink|京都市|Q34600}}{{jalink|北区 (京都市)|Q1154970|北区}}紫竹下竹殿町47 |位置 = {{Coord|35|3|7.4|N|135|44|52.7|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=久我神社}} |祭神 = {{jalink|賀茂建角身命|Q11634939}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（小）
{{jalink|賀茂別雷神社|Q700448}}境外摂社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = 一間社{{jalink|流造|Q210684}}{{jalink|檜皮葺|Q90373477}} |別名 = 氏神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|4月1日|Q2510}}・{{jalink|11月1日|Q2997}}{{Sfn|上賀茂神社（学生社）|2003|pp=51-52}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto city|label=久我神社|position=bottom|width=180|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} [[File:Kuga-jinja (Kyoto) nishi-torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|西鳥居]] '''久我神社'''（くがじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|京都府|Q120730}}{{jalink|京都市|Q34600}}{{jalink|北区_(京都市)|Q1154970|北区}}紫竹下竹殿町にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}で、現在は{{jalink|賀茂別雷神社|Q700448}}（上賀茂神社）の境外{{jalink|摂社|Q135009904}}（第八摂社）。 旧称は「'''氏神社'''」。上賀茂神社の西約1キロメートルの地に鎮座する{{Sfn|上賀茂神社（学生社）|2003|pp=51-52}}。 === 祭神 === 祭神は次の1柱{{Sfn|上賀茂神社（学生社）|2003|pp=51-52}}。 * '''{{jalink|賀茂建角身命|Q11634939}}''' （かもたけつぬみのみこと） *: {{jalink|賀茂県主氏|Q2975287|賀茂県主}}（賀茂氏）の祖神。上賀茂神社祭神の{{jalink|賀茂別雷命|Q11634943}}（かもわけいかづちのみこと）の祖父にあたり、{{jalink|神武天皇|Q200188}}の{{jalink|神武東征|Q11590444|東征}}で先導した{{jalink|八咫烏|Q745463}}（やたがらす）と同一視される。 古くより社名を「氏神社」を称することから、祭神は賀茂氏祖先神の賀茂建角身命とされるが、上賀茂神社文書によれば{{jalink|近世|Q5308718}}には{{jalink|国之常立神|Q478504|国常立尊}}等の異説も存在した{{Sfn|久我神社（式内社）|1979}}。 === 歴史 === {{座標一覧}} === 創建 === [[File:Koga-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|久我神社（京都市{{jalink|伏見区|Q1197771}}）}}]] 創建は不詳。当社は、{{jalink|賀茂県主氏|Q2975287}}が{{jalink|大和国|Q907495|大和}}から{{jalink|山城国|Q749276|山城}}に移住するに際して祀った社の1つと見られている{{Sfn|久我神社（神々）|1985}}。『{{jalink|風土記|Q1194534|山城国風土記}}』逸文『釈日本紀』巻9所引『山城国風土記』逸文。によると、賀茂建角身命は神武天皇の東征に功を成したのち、大和から「山代の国の岡田の賀茂」、「葛野川（{{jalink|桂川 (淀川水系) |Q2909307|桂川}}）と賀茂河（{{jalink|鴨川 (京都府)|Q27218|鴨川}}）との会う所」、「久我の国の北の山基」の順に遷座したといい、それぞれの地には現在も賀茂氏の氏神が祀られている{{Sfn|久我神社（神々）|1985}}。それぞれの比定地は次の通り。 # 山代の国の岡田の賀茂 - 現在の京都府{{jalink|木津川市|Q990352}}加茂町。{{jalink|式内社}}の{{jalink|岡田鴨神社|Q11473738}}（{{Coord|34|45|41.55|N|135|52|34.92|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|name=1. 岡田鴨神社}}）が鎮座。 # 葛野川と賀茂河との会う所 - 現在の京都府京都市{{jalink|伏見区|Q1197771}}。式内社の久何神社（現・{{jalink|久我神社 (京都市伏見区)||久我神社}}、{{Coord|34|56|26.37|N|135|43|48.94|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|name=2. 久何神社（現・久我神社）}}）が鎮座。 # 久我の国の北の山基 - 当社付近。 「{{jalink|山背久我国造||久我（国）}}」の地名については、当社一帯の古称であるが賀茂氏勢力の広がりにつれて衰えたとする説がある一方、本来は久何神社（久我神社）のある{{jalink|乙訓郡|Q376858}}の呼称で当地に踏襲されたとする説や、乙訓郡ではなく南山城の{{jalink|紀伊郡|Q11604966}}を本来の呼称と見る説が挙げられている{{Sfn|久我神社（平凡社）|1979}}{{Sfn|久我神社（神々）|1985}}。 当社が現在地に祀られたことに関しては、付近に賀茂建角身命の墳墓があったためとする伝承があるが、明らかではない{{Sfn|上賀茂神社（学生社）|2003|pp=51-52}}。 === 概史 === {{jalink|六国史|Q1151168|国史}}では、{{jalink|貞観 (日本)|Q1075635|貞観}}元年（{{jalink|859年|Q23897}}）『日本三代実録』貞観元年（859年）正月27日条。に「久我神」の{{jalink|神階|Q11591025}}が正六位上から従五位下へ昇叙されたと見える{{Sfn|久我神社（神々）|1985}}。また、{{jalink|延長 (元号)|Q1327274|延長}}5年（{{jalink|927年|Q30470}}）成立の『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}では{{jalink|山城国|Q749276}}{{jalink|愛宕郡|Q10981345}}に「久我神社」として{{jalink|式内社}}の記載が見え、これらの社は当社に比定される{{Sfn|久我神社（神々）|1985}}。 当社は賀茂社の『嘉元年中行事』において末社としての記載が見えることから、{{jalink|平安時代|Q193292}}後期頃から賀茂社の末社に列したと推測されている{{Sfn|久我神社（平凡社）|1979}}。また、社名も{{jalink|鎌倉時代|Q236205}}以降は「氏神社」と称していた境内説明板。。ただし祭祀のうえでは、{{jalink|中世|Q12554}}にはすでに賀茂氏の氏神としての性格は薄れ、郷民の社としての性格を強めている{{Sfn|久我神社（式内社）|1979}}。 {{jalink|宝暦|Q1194397}}4年（{{jalink|1754年|Q7623}}）の『{{jalink|山城名跡巡行志}}』では「紫竹の北端には大宮の森、一名を栢の森と呼ぶ。古社があり、{{jalink|鳥居|Q234731}}は西向きで拝殿は南向き。」という記述が見える。 {{jalink|近世|Q5308718}}以降、当社は主に「氏神社」や「大宮」と称されていたが、{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}）に社名を「久我神社」に改めて現在に至っている{{Sfn|久我神社（神々）|1985}}。 === 神階 === * {{jalink|貞観 (日本)|Q1075635|貞観}}元年（{{jalink|859年|Q23897}}）1月27日、正六位上から従五位下 （『{{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981}}』） - 表記は「久我神」。 === 境内 === [[File:Kuga-jinja2.JPG|thumb|200px|ケヤキの切り株
{{Smaller|樹齢は600年といわれる{{Sfn|上賀茂神社（学生社）|2003|pp=51-52}}。}}]] {{jalink|貞享|Q1146221}}3年（{{jalink|1686年|Q7664}}）の『{{jalink|雍州府志|Q18460389}}』では境内は「大宮の森」と俗称されるが、敷地内には現在もその面影を伝える{{jalink|ケヤキ|Q920344}}の巨木を残している。由緒のうえでも賀茂氏の移住伝承を現在に伝えており、京都の歴史を明らかにする環境を良好に残しているとして、境内は京都市指定史跡に指定されている。 現在の本殿・拝殿は{{jalink|寛永|Q1192913}}5年（{{jalink|1628年|Q6840}}）の造営{{Sfn|上賀茂神社（学生社）|2003|pp=51-52}}。本殿は一間社{{jalink|流造|Q210684}}の形式で、{{jalink|檜皮葺|Q90373477}}の屋根であり、上賀茂神社の他摂社と同じ造りになる。また拝殿は梁間一間・桁行二間の{{jalink|切妻造|Q13308575}}妻入で、屋根は同じく檜皮葺である。 なお、創建当時より久我神社は「大宮」と称されたとして、一帯の地域名が「大宮」（大宮郷）、門前の通り名は「大宮通」と定められたとする伝えがある。その通りとは{{jalink|大徳寺通|Q11435442}}で、地元では今日でも「旧大宮通」と称されている。 === 祭事 === * 春祭 （{{jalink|4月1日|Q2510}}） * {{jalink|八朔祭}} （{{jalink|9月1日|Q2859}}） * 秋祭 （{{jalink|11月1日|Q2997}}） * 神幸祭 （{{jalink|11月3日|Q2995}}） - 旧大宮郷四学区（大宮・待鳳・紫竹・元町）の氏子町内を{{jalink|神輿|Q979940}}が巡行する。 春秋の例祭では「牽馬の儀（ひきうまのぎ）」として、上賀茂神社の神馬が本殿を3周する神事が行われる。 === 文化財 === === 京都市指定史跡 === * 久我神社境内 - 昭和62年5月1日指定[https://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/bunshi/page/0000005881.html 京都市指定・登録文化財－史跡－北区]（京都市ホームページ「京都市情報館」）。。 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ;原典 {{Reflist|group="原"}} === 参考文献 === * 神社由緒書 * 境内説明板（京都市設置）　 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=久我神社|title={{jalink|日本歴史地名大系|Q17213509}} 27 京都市の地名|publisher={{jalink|平凡社|Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harvid|久我神社（平凡社）|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=式内社研究会編|author=泉谷康夫|chapter=久我神社|year=1979|title=式内社調査報告 第1巻|publisher={{jalink|皇學館大学|Q1797584}}出版部|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|久我神社（式内社）|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor={{jalink|谷川健一|Q2392596}}編|author=源城政好|year=1986|chapter=久我神社|title=日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 5 山城・近江|publisher={{jalink|白水社|Q4346470}}|isbn=4560022151|ref={{Harvid|久我神社（神々）|1985}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=建内光儀|chapter=摂末社の由緒|year=2003|title=上賀茂神社|publisher={{jalink|学生社|Q11448819}}|isbn=4311407203|ref={{Harvid|上賀茂神社（学生社）|2003}}}} * 『京都の地名検証』（京都地名研究会編、勉誠出版、2005年） * 『探訪 京都・上賀茂と二つの鞍馬街道－その今昔』（西村勁一郎(個人)、2008年） === 関連項目 === {{Commonscat|Kuga-jinja}} * {{jalink|賀茂別雷神社|Q700448}}（上賀茂神社） * {{jalink|大宮通|Q11434468}}（新大宮通） * {{jalink|航空神社|Q30319396}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くかしんしや きようとしきたく}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuga Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kuga Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:京都市の神社]] [[Category:京都市北区の歴史]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:賀茂県主氏|社くかしんしや きようとしきたく]] [[Category:京都市北区の建築物]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

Shrine building
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=280|coord={{coord|35|3|7.4|N|135|44|52.7|E}}}} |Location = {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}{{jalink|Kyoto City|Q34600}}{{jalink|Kita Ward (Kyoto City)|Q1154970|Kita Ward}}47 Shichiku Shimotakedonomachi |Location = {{Coord|35|3|7.4|N|135|44|52.7|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kuga Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Kamo Kenkaku no Mikoto|Q11634939}} |Divinity = |Shaku = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
{{jalink|Kamobetsu Raijinja|Q700448}}Kyotogaisetsusha |Foundation = unknown |Main shrine = Ikkensha {{jalink|Ryuzo|Q210684}}{{jalink|cypress bark roof|Q90373477}} |Alternative name = Uji Shrine |Fudasho, etc. =|Regular Festival = {{jalink|April 1st|Q2510}}・{{jalink|November 1st|Q2997}}{{Sfn|Kamigamo Shrine (Student Shrine)|2003|pp=51-52}} |Shinto ritual = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto city|label=Kuga Shrine|position=bottom|width=180|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} [[File:Kuga-jinja (Kyoto) nishi-torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nishi Torii]] '''Kuga Shrine'' (Kugajinja) is located in {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}{{jalink|Kyoto City|Q34600}}{{jalink|Kita Ward_(Kyoto City)|Q1154970|Kita Ward}} Shichiku-Shimotakedono-cho.{{jalink|Shrine|Q845945}}. {{jalink|Shikinai Shrine}}, currently outside the precincts of {{jalink|Kamobetsu Rai Shrine|Q700448}} (Kamigamo Shrine) {{jalink|Setsusha|Q135009904}} (8th Setsusha). The former name was ``''Uji Shrine''''. {{Sfn|Kamigamo Shrine (Student Shrine)|2003|pp=51-52}} is located about 1 km west of Kamigamo Shrine. === Enshrined God === The following deity is enshrined: {{Sfn|Kamigamo Shrine (Student Shrine)|2003|pp=51-52}}. * '''{{jalink|Kamo Kenkaku no Mikoto|Q11634939}}''' (Kamotake Tsunumi Mikoto) *: Ancestor god of {{jalink|Kamo Prefectural Lord|Q2975287|Kamo Prefectural Lord}} (Kamo clan). He was the grandfather of Kamigamo Shrine's enshrined deity, {{jalink|Kamobetsu Rai-no-Mikoto|Q11634943}} (Kamowakeikazuchi no Mikoto), {{jalink|Emperor Jimmu|Q2001 It is identified with {{jalink|Yatagarasu|Q745463}} (Yatagarasu) who led the {{jalink|Jinmu Tosei|Q11590444|Tosei}} in 88}}. Since ancient times the company name has been called ``Uji Shrine'', the enshrined deity is said to be Kamo Takekaku no Mikoto, the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan, but according to Kamigamo Shrine documents, {{jalink|Early modern |Q53 08718}} also had different theories such as {{jalink|Kuni no Tokodatejin|Q478504|Kuni no Tokodateson}} {{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1979}}. === History === {{List of coordinates}} === Founding === [[File:Koga-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Kuga Shrine (Kyoto City{{jalink|Fushimi Ward|Q1197771}})}}]] Founding date is unknown. Our company is believed to be one of the shrines {{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Gods)|1985}} that was enshrined by {{jalink|Kamo Prefectural Lord|Q2975287}} when he moved from {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495|Yamato}} to {{jalink|Yamashiro Province|Q749276|Yamashiro}}. "{{jalink|Fudoki|Q1194534|Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki}}" Isshu Isshu of "Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki" quoted in volume 9 of "Shaku Nihongi". According to , after Kamo Kenkakumi no Mikoto achieved great success in Emperor Jimmu's eastern expedition, he moved from Yamato to ``Kamo in Okada, Yamashiro Province'', ``Kazuno River ({{jalink|Katsura River (Yodo River system) |Q2909307|Katsura River}}) and Kamo River ({{jalink|Kamogawa River}). (Kyoto Prefecture) | Q27218 | Kamogawa}})" and "Yamamoto in the north of Kuga no Kuni", and the guardian deities of the Kamo clan are still enshrined at each location {{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Gods) | 1985}}. The specific locations for each location are as follows.# Kamo, Okada, Yamashiro no Kuni - present-day Kamo Town, Kyoto Prefecture {{jalink|Kizugawa City|Q990352}}. The {{jalink|Okada Kamo Shrine|Q11473738}} ({{Coord|34|45|41.55|N|135|52|34.92|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|name=1. Okada Kamo Shrine}}) of {{jalink|Shikinai Shrine}} is enshrined here. # Where the Kazuno River meets the Kamo River - present-day Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture {{jalink|Fushimi Ward|Q1197771}}. The Shikinai shrine, Kuga Shrine (currently {{jalink|Kuga Shrine (Fushimi Ward, Kyoto City)||Kuga Shrine}}, {{Coord|34|56|26.37|N|135|43|48.94|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|name=2. Kuga Shrine (currently Kuga Shrine)}}) is located there. # Yamamoto in the north of Kuga no Kuni - near our company. Regarding the place name "{{jalink|Yamase Kuga Kunizo||Kuga (country)}}", there is a theory that it is an old name for the entire area, but it declined as the Kamo clan's influence spread, but it was originally the name of {{jalink|Otokuni District|Q376858}}, where Kuga Shrine (Kuga Shrine) is located. There is a theory that the name was followed in this area, and another theory that the original name is {{jalink|Kii-gun|Q11604966}} of Minamiyama Castle, not Otokuni-gun. There is a tradition that our company was enshrined at its current location because there was a tomb of Kenkaku Kamo no Mikoto nearby, but this is not clear {{Sfn|Kamigamo Shrine (Student Shrine)|2003|pp=51-52}} === Outline === In {{jalink|Rokukokushi|Q1151168|Kokushi}}, {{jalink|Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōgan}} 1st year ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}) ``Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' article on the 27th day of the first month of Jogan 1st year (859). In , it appears that the {{jalink|Shinkai|Q11591025}} of ``Kugagami'' was promoted from the upper rank of upper sixth rank to lower rank of junior fifth rank {{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. Also, {{jalink|extension (Era name)|Q1327274|Extended}} 5 years ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}) "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shinmyocho}} link|Yamashiro Province|Q749276}}{{jalink|Atago District|Q10981345}} {{jalink|Shikiuchi-sha}} is listed as "Kuga Shrine", and these shrines are compared to our company {{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Gods)|1985}}.Since our company is listed as a subsidiary shrine in Kamo Shrine's ``Kagen Annual Events'', it is assumed that it was listed as a subsidiary shrine of Kamo Shrine from around the late {{jalink|Heian period|Q193292}}{{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}. In addition, the company name was also called "Uji Shrine" after {{jalink|Kamakura period|Q236205}}. . However, in terms of rituals, by {{jalink|Middle Ages|Q12554}}, the Kamo clan's character as a guardian deity had already faded, and its character as a shrine for the local people had strengthened {{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1979}}. In {{jalink|Horeki|Q1194397}} 4th year ({{jalink|1754|Q7623}}) "{{jalink|Yamashiro Famous Sites Pilgrimage}}" At the northern end of Shichiku, there is an old shrine called Omiya no Mori, which is called Haku no Mori, and you can see the description that {{jalink|torii|Q234731}} faces west and the hall of worship faces south. Since {{jalink|early modern times|Q5308718}}, our company was mainly called "Uji Shrine" or "Omiya", but {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096} In }5 ({{jalink|1872|Q7753}}), the company name was changed to "Kuga Shrine" and remains the current name {{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. === God level === * {{jalink|Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jogan}} January 27, 1st year ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), from upper 6th rank to lower rank of 5th rank (``{{jalink|Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}'') - Written as ``Kugagami.'' === Precincts === [[File:Kuga-jinja2.JPG|thumb|200px|A zelkova stump
{{Smaller|The tree is said to be 600 years old.{{Sfn|Kamigamo Shrine (Student Shrine)|2003|pp=51-52}}. }}]] In the ``{{jalink|Yongshu Fushi|Q18460389}}'' written in 1686|Q7664}}, the precincts are commonly referred to as the ``Omiya Forest,'' and there are still giant {{jalink|zelkova|Q920344}} trees on the premises that still bear traces of that past.. The temple grounds have been designated as a Kyoto City designated historic site, as the tradition of the migration of the Kamo clan has been passed down to the present day, and the environment that reveals the history of Kyoto has been preserved in good condition. The current main shrine and worship hall were built in {{jalink|Kanei|Q1192913}}5 ({{jalink|1628|Q6840}}){{Sfn|Kamigamo Shrine (Student Shrine)|2003|pp=51-52}}. The main shrine is in the style of Ikken-sha {{jalink|Nagazuzukuri|Q210684}}, with a {{jalink|cypress bark roof|Q90373477}} roof, and is the same structure as other shrines of Kamigamo Shrine. The worship hall has a {{jalink|gabled structure|Q13308575}} gable with one beam and two beams, and the roof is also thatched with cypress bark.Furthermore, there is a legend that Kuga Shrine was called ``Omiya'' from the time of its establishment, and that the name of the area was ``Omiya'' (Omiya-go) and the street name in front of the gate was ``Omiya-dori.'' That street is {{jalink|Daitokuji-dori|Q11435442}}, which locals still refer to as ``Old Omiya-dori'' today. === Festival === * Spring Festival ({{jalink|April 1st|Q2510}}) * {{jalink|Hassaku Festival}} ({{jalink|September 1st|Q2859}}) * Autumn Festival ({{jalink|November 1st|Q2997}}) * Shinko Festival ({{jalink|November 3rd|Q2995}}) - {{jalink|Mikoshi|Mikoshi|Q979940}} parades through the shrine parishioners of the four former Omiya-go school districts (Omiya, Taiho, Shichiku, and Motomachi). During the spring and autumn festivals, a ritual called ``Hikiuma-nogi'' is held in which Kamigamo Shrine's sacred horse circles the main shrine three times. === Cultural assets === === Kyoto City designated historic site === * Kuga Shrine Precincts - Designated on May 1, 1988 [https://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/bunshi/page/0000005881.html Kyoto City Designated/Registered Cultural Property - Historic Site - Kita Ward] (Kyoto City website "Kyoto City Information Center"). . === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} ;Original text {{Reflist|group="Original"}} === References === * Shrine history book * Precinct information board (installed in Kyoto City) * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Kuga Shrine|title={{jalink|Japanese historical place name system|Q17213509}} 27 Kyoto city place name|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harvid|Kuga Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=Shikiuchisha Research Group|author=Yasuo Izumiya|chapter=Kuga Shrine|year=1979|title=Shikiuchisha Investigation Report Volume 1|publisher={{jalink|Kogakkan University|Q1797584}}Publishing Department|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Kuga Shrine (Shikiuchisha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor={{jalink|Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596}}edited|author=Masayoshi Genjo|year=1986|chapter=Kuga Shrine|title=Japanese Gods -Shrines and Sacred Sites- 5 Yamashiro/Omi|publisher={{jalink|Hakusuisha|Q4346470}}|isbn=4560022151|ref={{Harvid|Kuga Shrine (Gods)|1985}}}}* {{Cite book|Japanese book|author=Mitsugi Kennai|chapter=The history of Setsusesha|year=2003|title=Kamigamo Shrine|publisher={{jalink|Kamikamo Shrine|Q11448819}}|isbn=4311407203|ref={{Harvid|Kamigamo Shrine (Kamikazesha)|2003}}}} * “Kyoto Place Name Verification” (edited by Kyoto Place Name Research Group, Bensei Publishing, 2005) * “Exploring Kyoto, Kamigamo and the Two Kurama Highways – Past and Present” (Keiichiro Nishimura (individual), 2008) === Related items === {{Commonscat|Kuga-jinja}} * {{jalink|Kamobetsu Rai Shrine|Q700448}} (Kamigamo Shrine) * {{jalink|Omiya-dori|Q11434468}} (Shin-Omiya-dori) * {{jalink|Air Shrine|Q30319396}} {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kukashinshiya Kiyoto Shitoku}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuga Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kuga Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:京都市の神社]] [[Category:京都市北区の歴史]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:賀茂県主氏|社くかしんしや きようとしきたく]] [[Category:京都市北区の建築物]] There is a tradition that our company was enshrined at its current location because there was a tomb of Kenkaku Kamo no Mikoto nearby, but this is not clear {{Sfn|Kamigamo Shrine (Student Shrine)|2003|pp=51-52}} === Outline === In {{jalink|Rokukokushi|Q1151168|Kokushi}}, {{jalink|Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōgan}} 1st year ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}) ``Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' article on the 27th day of the first month of Jogan 1st year (859). In , it appears that the {{jalink|Shinkai|Q11591025}} of ``Kugagami'' was promoted from the upper rank of upper sixth rank to lower rank of junior fifth rank {{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. Also, {{jalink|extension (Era name)|Q1327274|Extended}} 5 years ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}) "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shinmyocho}} link|Yamashiro Province|Q749276}}{{jalink|Atago District|Q10981345}} {{jalink|Shikiuchi-sha}} is listed as "Kuga Shrine", and these shrines are compared to our company {{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Gods)|1985}}.Since our company is listed as a subsidiary shrine in Kamo Shrine's ``Kagen Annual Events'', it is assumed that it was listed as a subsidiary shrine of Kamo Shrine from around the late {{jalink|Heian period|Q193292}}{{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}. In addition, the company name was also called "Uji Shrine" after {{jalink|Kamakura period|Q236205}}. . However, in terms of rituals, by {{jalink|Middle Ages|Q12554}}, the Kamo clan's character as a guardian deity had already faded, and its character as a shrine for the local people had strengthened {{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1979}}. In {{jalink|Horeki|Q1194397}} 4th year ({{jalink|1754|Q7623}}) "{{jalink|Yamashiro Famous Sites Pilgrimage}}" At the northern end of Shichiku, there is an old shrine called Omiya no Mori, which is called Haku no Mori, and you can see the description that {{jalink|torii|Q234731}} faces west and the hall of worship faces south. Since {{jalink|early modern times|Q5308718}}, our company was mainly called "Uji Shrine" or "Omiya", but {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096} In }5 ({{jalink|1872|Q7753}}), the company name was changed to "Kuga Shrine" and remains the current name {{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. === God level === * {{jalink|Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jogan}} January 27, 1st year ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), from upper 6th rank to lower rank of 5th rank (``{{jalink|Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}'') - Written as ``Kugagami.'' === Precincts === [[File:Kuga-jinja2.JPG|thumb|200px|A zelkova stump
{{Smaller|The tree is said to be 600 years old.{{Sfn|Kamigamo Shrine (Student Shrine)|2003|pp=51-52}}. }}]] In the ``{{jalink|Yongshu Fushi|Q18460389}}'' written in 1686|Q7664}}, the precincts are commonly referred to as the ``Omiya Forest,'' and there are still giant {{jalink|zelkova|Q920344}} trees on the premises that still bear traces of that past.. The temple grounds have been designated as a Kyoto City designated historic site, as the tradition of the migration of the Kamo clan has been passed down to the present day, and the environment that reveals the history of Kyoto has been preserved in good condition. The current main shrine and worship hall were built in {{jalink|Kanei|Q1192913}}5 ({{jalink|1628|Q6840}}){{Sfn|Kamigamo Shrine (Student Shrine)|2003|pp=51-52}}. The main shrine is in the style of Ikken-sha {{jalink|Nagazuzukuri|Q210684}}, with a {{jalink|cypress bark roof|Q90373477}} roof, and is the same structure as other shrines of Kamigamo Shrine. The worship hall has a {{jalink|gabled structure|Q13308575}} gable with one beam and two beams, and the roof is also thatched with cypress bark.Furthermore, there is a legend that Kuga Shrine was called ``Omiya'' from the time of its establishment, and that the name of the area was ``Omiya'' (Omiya-go) and the street name in front of the gate was ``Omiya-dori.'' That street is {{jalink|Daitokuji-dori|Q11435442}}, which locals still refer to as ``Old Omiya-dori'' today. === Festival === * Spring Festival ({{jalink|April 1st|Q2510}}) * {{jalink|Hassaku Festival}} ({{jalink|September 1st|Q2859}}) * Autumn Festival ({{jalink|November 1st|Q2997}}) * Shinko Festival ({{jalink|November 3rd|Q2995}}) - {{jalink|Mikoshi|Mikoshi|Q979940}} parades through the shrine parishioners of the four former Omiya-go school districts (Omiya, Taiho, Shichiku, and Motomachi). During the spring and autumn festivals, a ritual called ``Hikiuma-nogi'' is held in which Kamigamo Shrine's sacred horse circles the main shrine three times. === Cultural assets === === Kyoto City designated historic site === * Kuga Shrine Precincts - Designated on May 1, 1988 [https://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/bunshi/page/0000005881.html Kyoto City Designated/Registered Cultural Property - Historic Site - Kita Ward] (Kyoto City website "Kyoto City Information Center"). . === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} ;Original text {{Reflist|group="Original"}} === References === * Shrine history book * Precinct information board (installed in Kyoto City) * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Kuga Shrine|title={{jalink|Japanese historical place name system|Q17213509}} 27 Kyoto city place name|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harvid|Kuga Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=Shikiuchisha Research Group|author=Yasuo Izumiya|chapter=Kuga Shrine|year=1979|title=Shikiuchisha Investigation Report Volume 1|publisher={{jalink|Kogakkan University|Q1797584}}Publishing Department|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Kuga Shrine (Shikiuchisha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor={{jalink|Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596}}edited|author=Masayoshi Genjo|year=1986|chapter=Kuga Shrine|title=Japanese Gods -Shrines and Sacred Sites- 5 Yamashiro/Omi|publisher={{jalink|Hakusuisha|Q4346470}}|isbn=4560022151|ref={{Harvid|Kuga Shrine (Gods)|1985}}}}* {{Cite book|Japanese book|author=Mitsugi Kennai|chapter=The history of Setsusesha|year=2003|title=Kamigamo Shrine|publisher={{jalink|Kamikamo Shrine|Q11448819}}|isbn=4311407203|ref={{Harvid|Kamigamo Shrine (Kamikazesha)|2003}}}} * “Kyoto Place Name Verification” (edited by Kyoto Place Name Research Group, Bensei Publishing, 2005) * “Exploring Kyoto, Kamigamo and the Two Kurama Highways – Past and Present” (Keiichiro Nishimura (individual), 2008) === Related items === {{Commonscat|Kuga-jinja}} * {{jalink|Kamobetsu Rai Shrine|Q700448}} (Kamigamo Shrine) * {{jalink|Omiya-dori|Q11434468}} (Shin-Omiya-dori) * {{jalink|Air Shrine|Q30319396}} {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kukashinshiya Kiyoto Shitoku}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuga Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kuga Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:京都市の神社]] [[Category:京都市北区の歴史]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:賀茂県主氏|社くかしんしや きようとしきたく]] [[Category:京都市北区の建築物]] category|Category:Kuga-jinja}} [[JA:久我神社 (京都市北区)]] {{wikidata link|Q11369376}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Kuga Shrine |Image = [[File:Kuga-jinja (Kyoto) shaden.JPG|250px]]
Shrine building
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=280|coord={{coord|35|3|7.4|N|135|44|52.7|E}}}} |Location = {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}{{jalink|Kyoto City|Q34600}}{{jalink|Kita Ward (Kyoto City)|Q1154970|Kita Ward}}47 Shichiku Shimotakedonomachi |Location = {{Coord|35|3|7.4|N|135|44|52.7|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kuga Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Kamo Kenkaku no Mikoto|Q11634939}} |Divinity = |Shaku = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
{{jalink|Kamobetsu Raijinja|Q700448}}Kyotogaisetsusha |Foundation = unknown |Main shrine = Ikkensha {{jalink|Ryuzo|Q210684}}{{jalink|cypress bark roof|Q90373477}} |Alternative name = Uji Shrine |Fudasho, etc. =|Regular Festival = {{jalink|April 1st|Q2510}}・{{jalink|November 1st|Q2997}}{{Sfn|Kamigamo Shrine (Student Shrine)|2003|pp=51-52}} |Shinto ritual = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto city|label=Kuga Shrine|position=bottom|width=180|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} [[File:Kuga-jinja (Kyoto) nishi-torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nishi Torii]] '''Kuga Shrine'' (Kugajinja) is located in {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}{{jalink|Kyoto City|Q34600}}{{jalink|Kita Ward_(Kyoto City)|Q1154970|Kita Ward}} Shichiku-Shimotakedono-cho.{{jalink|Shrine|Q845945}}. {{jalink|Shikinai Shrine}}, currently outside the precincts of {{jalink|Kamobetsu Rai Shrine|Q700448}} (Kamigamo Shrine) {{jalink|Setsusha|Q135009904}} (8th Setsusha). The former name was ``''Uji Shrine''''. {{Sfn|Kamigamo Shrine (Student Shrine)|2003|pp=51-52}} is located about 1 km west of Kamigamo Shrine. === Enshrined God === The following deity is enshrined: {{Sfn|Kamigamo Shrine (Student Shrine)|2003|pp=51-52}}. * '''{{jalink|Kamo Kenkaku no Mikoto|Q11634939}}''' (Kamotake Tsunumi Mikoto) *: Ancestor god of {{jalink|Kamo Prefectural Lord|Q2975287|Kamo Prefectural Lord}} (Kamo clan). He was the grandfather of Kamigamo Shrine's enshrined deity, {{jalink|Kamobetsu Rai-no-Mikoto|Q11634943}} (Kamowakeikazuchi no Mikoto), {{jalink|Emperor Jimmu|Q2001 It is identified with {{jalink|Yatagarasu|Q745463}} (Yatagarasu) who led the {{jalink|Jinmu Tosei|Q11590444|Tosei}} in 88}}. Since ancient times the company name has been called ``Uji Shrine'', the enshrined deity is said to be Kamo Takekaku no Mikoto, the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan, but according to Kamigamo Shrine documents, {{jalink|Early modern |Q53 08718}} also had different theories such as {{jalink|Kuni no Tokodatejin|Q478504|Kuni no Tokodateson}} {{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1979}}. === History === {{List of coordinates}} === Founding === [[File:Koga-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Kuga Shrine (Kyoto City{{jalink|Fushimi Ward|Q1197771}})}}]] Founding date is unknown. Our company is believed to be one of the shrines {{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Gods)|1985}} that was enshrined by {{jalink|Kamo Prefectural Lord|Q2975287}} when he moved from {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495|Yamato}} to {{jalink|Yamashiro Province|Q749276|Yamashiro}}. "{{jalink|Fudoki|Q1194534|Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki}}" Isshu Isshu of "Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki" quoted in volume 9 of "Shaku Nihongi". According to , after Kamo Kenkakumi no Mikoto achieved great success in Emperor Jimmu's eastern expedition, he moved from Yamato to ``Kamo in Okada, Yamashiro Province'', ``Kazuno River ({{jalink|Katsura River (Yodo River system) |Q2909307|Katsura River}}) and Kamo River ({{jalink|Kamogawa River}). (Kyoto Prefecture) | Q27218 | Kamogawa}})" and "Yamamoto in the north of Kuga no Kuni", and the guardian deities of the Kamo clan are still enshrined at each location {{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Gods) | 1985}}. The specific locations for each location are as follows.# Kamo, Okada, Yamashiro no Kuni - present-day Kamo Town, Kyoto Prefecture {{jalink|Kizugawa City|Q990352}}. The {{jalink|Okada Kamo Shrine|Q11473738}} ({{Coord|34|45|41.55|N|135|52|34.92|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|name=1. Okada Kamo Shrine}}) of {{jalink|Shikinai Shrine}} is enshrined here. # Where the Kazuno River meets the Kamo River - present-day Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture {{jalink|Fushimi Ward|Q1197771}}. The Shikinai shrine, Kuga Shrine (currently {{jalink|Kuga Shrine (Fushimi Ward, Kyoto City)||Kuga Shrine}}, {{Coord|34|56|26.37|N|135|43|48.94|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|name=2. Kuga Shrine (currently Kuga Shrine)}}) is located there. # Yamamoto in the north of Kuga no Kuni - near our company. Regarding the place name "{{jalink|Yamase Kuga Kunizo||Kuga (country)}}", there is a theory that it is an old name for the entire area, but it declined as the Kamo clan's influence spread, but it was originally the name of {{jalink|Otokuni District|Q376858}}, where Kuga Shrine (Kuga Shrine) is located. There is a theory that the name was followed in this area, and another theory that the original name is {{jalink|Kii-gun|Q11604966}} of Minamiyama Castle, not Otokuni-gun. There is a tradition that our company was enshrined at its current location because there was a tomb of Kenkaku Kamo no Mikoto nearby, but this is not clear {{Sfn|Kamigamo Shrine (Student Shrine)|2003|pp=51-52}} === Outline === In {{jalink|Rokukokushi|Q1151168|Kokushi}}, {{jalink|Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōgan}} 1st year ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}) ``Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' article on the 27th day of the first month of Jogan 1st year (859). In , it appears that the {{jalink|Shinkai|Q11591025}} of ``Kugagami'' was promoted from the upper rank of upper sixth rank to lower rank of junior fifth rank {{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. In {{jalink|Horeki|Q1194397}} 4th year ({{jalink|1754|Q7623}}) "{{jalink|Yamashiro Famous Sites Pilgrimage}}" At the northern end of Shichiku, there is an old shrine called Omiya no Mori, which is called Haku no Mori, and you can see the description that {{jalink|torii|Q234731}} faces west and the hall of worship faces south. Since {{jalink|early modern times|Q5308718}}, our company was mainly called "Uji Shrine" or "Omiya", but {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096} In }5 ({{jalink|1872|Q7753}}), the company name was changed to "Kuga Shrine" and remains the current name {{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. === God level === * {{jalink|Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jogan}} January 27, 1st year ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), from upper 6th rank to lower rank of 5th rank (``{{jalink|Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}'') - Written as ``Kugagami.'' === Precincts === [[File:Kuga-jinja2.JPG|thumb|200px|A zelkova stump

category|Category:Kuga-jinja}} [[JA:久我神社 (京都市北区)]] {{wikidata link|Q11369376}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Kuga Shrine |Image = [[File:Kuga-jinja (Kyoto) shaden.JPG|250px]]
Shrine building
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=280|coord={{coord|35|3|7.4|N|135|44|52.7|E}}}} |Location = {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}{{jalink|Kyoto City|Q34600}}{{jalink|Kita Ward (Kyoto City)|Q1154970|Kita Ward}}47 Shichiku Shimotakedonomachi |Location = {{Coord|35|3|7.4|N|135|44|52.7|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kuga Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Kamo Kenkaku no Mikoto|Q11634939}} |Divinity = |Shaku = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
{{jalink|Kamobetsu Raijinja|Q700448}}Kyotogaisetsusha |Foundation = unknown |Main shrine = Ikkensha {{jalink|Ryuzo|Q210684}}{{jalink|cypress bark roof|Q90373477}} |Alternative name = Uji Shrine |Fudasho, etc. =|Regular Festival = {{jalink|April 1st|Q2510}}・{{jalink|November 1st|Q2997}}{{Sfn|Kamigamo Shrine (Student Shrine)|2003|pp=51-52}} |Shinto ritual = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto city|label=Kuga Shrine|position=bottom|width=180|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} [[File:Kuga-jinja (Kyoto) nishi-torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nishi Torii]] '''Kuga Shrine'' (Kugajinja) is located in {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}{{jalink|Kyoto City|Q34600}}{{jalink|Kita Ward_(Kyoto City)|Q1154970|Kita Ward}} Shichiku-Shimotakedono-cho.{{jalink|Shrine|Q845945}}. {{jalink|Shikinai Shrine}}, currently outside the precincts of {{jalink|Kamobetsu Rai Shrine|Q700448}} (Kamigamo Shrine) {{jalink|Setsusha|Q135009904}} (8th Setsusha). The former name was ``''Uji Shrine''''. {{Sfn|Kamigamo Shrine (Student Shrine)|2003|pp=51-52}} is located about 1 km west of Kamigamo Shrine. === Enshrined God === The following deity is enshrined: {{Sfn|Kamigamo Shrine (Student Shrine)|2003|pp=51-52}}. * '''{{jalink|Kamo Kenkaku no Mikoto|Q11634939}}''' (Kamotake Tsunumi Mikoto) *: Ancestor god of {{jalink|Kamo Prefectural Lord|Q2975287|Kamo Prefectural Lord}} (Kamo clan). He was the grandfather of Kamigamo Shrine's enshrined deity, {{jalink|Kamobetsu Rai-no-Mikoto|Q11634943}} (Kamowakeikazuchi no Mikoto), {{jalink|Emperor Jimmu|Q2001 It is identified with {{jalink|Yatagarasu|Q745463}} (Yatagarasu) who led the {{jalink|Jinmu Tosei|Q11590444|Tosei}} in 88}}. Since ancient times the company name has been called ``Uji Shrine'', the enshrined deity is said to be Kamo Takekaku no Mikoto, the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan, but according to Kamigamo Shrine documents, {{jalink|Early modern |Q53 08718}} also had different theories such as {{jalink|Kuni no Tokodatejin|Q478504|Kuni no Tokodateson}} {{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1979}}. === History === {{List of coordinates}} === Founding === [[File:Koga-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Kuga Shrine (Kyoto City{{jalink|Fushimi Ward|Q1197771}})}}]] Founding date is unknown. Our company is believed to be one of the shrines {{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Gods)|1985}} that was enshrined by {{jalink|Kamo Prefectural Lord|Q2975287}} when he moved from {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495|Yamato}} to {{jalink|Yamashiro Province|Q749276|Yamashiro}}. "{{jalink|Fudoki|Q1194534|Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki}}" Isshu Isshu of "Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki" quoted in volume 9 of "Shaku Nihongi". According to , after Kamo Kenkakumi no Mikoto achieved great success in Emperor Jimmu's eastern expedition, he moved from Yamato to ``Kamo in Okada, Yamashiro Province'', ``Kazuno River ({{jalink|Katsura River (Yodo River system) |Q2909307|Katsura River}}) and Kamo River ({{jalink|Kamogawa River}). (Kyoto Prefecture) | Q27218 | Kamogawa}})" and "Yamamoto in the north of Kuga no Kuni", and the guardian deities of the Kamo clan are still enshrined at each location {{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Gods) | 1985}}. The specific locations for each location are as follows.# Kamo, Okada, Yamashiro no Kuni - present-day Kamo Town, Kyoto Prefecture {{jalink|Kizugawa City|Q990352}}. The {{jalink|Okada Kamo Shrine|Q11473738}} ({{Coord|34|45|41.55|N|135|52|34.92|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|name=1. Okada Kamo Shrine}}) of {{jalink|Shikinai Shrine}} is enshrined here. # Where the Kazuno River meets the Kamo River - present-day Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture {{jalink|Fushimi Ward|Q1197771}}. The Shikinai shrine, Kuga Shrine (currently {{jalink|Kuga Shrine (Fushimi Ward, Kyoto City)||Kuga Shrine}}, {{Coord|34|56|26.37|N|135|43|48.94|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|name=2. Kuga Shrine (currently Kuga Shrine)}}) is located there. # Yamamoto in the north of Kuga no Kuni - near our company. Regarding the place name "{{jalink|Yamase Kuga Kunizo||Kuga (country)}}", there is a theory that it is an old name for the entire area, but it declined as the Kamo clan's influence spread, but it was originally the name of {{jalink|Otokuni District|Q376858}}, where Kuga Shrine (Kuga Shrine) is located. There is a theory that the name was followed in this area, and another theory that the original name is {{jalink|Kii-gun|Q11604966}} of Minamiyama Castle, not Otokuni-gun. There is a tradition that our company was enshrined at its current location because there was a tomb of Kenkaku Kamo no Mikoto nearby, but this is not clear {{Sfn|Kamigamo Shrine (Student Shrine)|2003|pp=51-52}} === Outline === In {{jalink|Rokukokushi|Q1151168|Kokushi}}, {{jalink|Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōgan}} 1st year ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}) ``Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' article on the 27th day of the first month of Jogan 1st year (859). In , it appears that the {{jalink|Shinkai|Q11591025}} of ``Kugagami'' was promoted from the upper rank of upper sixth rank to lower rank of junior fifth rank {{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. Also, {{jalink|extension (Era name)|Q1327274|Extended}} 5 years ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}) "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shinmyocho}} link|Yamashiro Province|Q749276}}{{jalink|Atago District|Q10981345}} {{jalink|Shikiuchi-sha}} is listed as "Kuga Shrine", and these shrines are compared to our company {{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Gods)|1985}}.Since our company is listed as a subsidiary shrine in Kamo Shrine's ``Kagen Annual Events'', it is assumed that it was listed as a subsidiary shrine of Kamo Shrine from around the late {{jalink|Heian period|Q193292}}{{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}. In addition, the company name was also called "Uji Shrine" after {{jalink|Kamakura period|Q236205}}. . However, in terms of rituals, by {{jalink|Middle Ages|Q12554}}, the Kamo clan's character as a guardian deity had already faded, and its character as a shrine for the local people had strengthened {{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1979}}. In {{jalink|Horeki|Q1194397}} 4th year ({{jalink|1754|Q7623}}) "{{jalink|Yamashiro Famous Sites Pilgrimage}}" At the northern end of Shichiku, there is an old shrine called Omiya no Mori, which is called Haku no Mori, and you can see the description that {{jalink|torii|Q234731}} faces west and the hall of worship faces south. Since {{jalink|early modern times|Q5308718}}, our company was mainly called "Uji Shrine" or "Omiya", but {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096} In }5 ({{jalink|1872|Q7753}}), the company name was changed to "Kuga Shrine" and remains the current name {{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. === God level === * {{jalink|Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jogan}} January 27, 1st year ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), from upper 6th rank to lower rank of 5th rank (``{{jalink|Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}'') - Written as ``Kugagami.'' === Precincts === [[File:Kuga-jinja2.JPG|thumb|200px|A zelkova stump
{{Smaller|The tree is said to be 600 years old.{{Sfn|Kamigamo Shrine (Student Shrine)|2003|pp=51-52}}. }}]] In the ``{{jalink|Yongshu Fushi|Q18460389}}'' written in 1686|Q7664}}, the precincts are commonly referred to as the ``Omiya Forest,'' and there are still giant {{jalink|zelkova|Q920344}} trees on the premises that still bear traces of that past.. The temple grounds have been designated as a Kyoto City designated historic site, as the tradition of the migration of the Kamo clan has been passed down to the present day, and the environment that reveals the history of Kyoto has been preserved in good condition. The current main shrine and worship hall were built in {{jalink|Kanei|Q1192913}}5 ({{jalink|1628|Q6840}}){{Sfn|Kamigamo Shrine (Student Shrine)|2003|pp=51-52}}. The main shrine is in the style of Ikken-sha {{jalink|Nagazuzukuri|Q210684}}, with a {{jalink|cypress bark roof|Q90373477}} roof, and is the same structure as other shrines of Kamigamo Shrine. The worship hall has a {{jalink|gabled structure|Q13308575}} gable with one beam and two beams, and the roof is also thatched with cypress bark.Furthermore, there is a legend that Kuga Shrine was called ``Omiya'' from the time of its establishment, and that the name of the area was ``Omiya'' (Omiya-go) and the street name in front of the gate was ``Omiya-dori.'' That street is {{jalink|Daitokuji-dori|Q11435442}}, which locals still refer to as ``Old Omiya-dori'' today. === Festival === * Spring Festival ({{jalink|April 1st|Q2510}}) * {{jalink|Hassaku Festival}} ({{jalink|September 1st|Q2859}}) * Autumn Festival ({{jalink|November 1st|Q2997}}) * Shinko Festival ({{jalink|November 3rd|Q2995}}) - {{jalink|Mikoshi|Mikoshi|Q979940}} parades through the shrine parishioners of the four former Omiya-go school districts (Omiya, Taiho, Shichiku, and Motomachi). During the spring and autumn festivals, a ritual called ``Hikiuma-nogi'' is held in which Kamigamo Shrine's sacred horse circles the main shrine three times. === Cultural assets === === Kyoto City designated historic site === * Kuga Shrine Precincts - Designated on May 1, 1988 [https://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/bunshi/page/0000005881.html Kyoto City Designated/Registered Cultural Property - Historic Site - Kita Ward] (Kyoto City website "Kyoto City Information Center"). . === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} ;Original text {{Reflist|group="Original"}} === References === * Shrine history book * Precinct information board (installed in Kyoto City) * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Kuga Shrine|title={{jalink|Japanese historical place name system|Q17213509}} 27 Kyoto city place name|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harvid|Kuga Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=Shikiuchisha Research Group|author=Yasuo Izumiya|chapter=Kuga Shrine|year=1979|title=Shikiuchisha Investigation Report Volume 1|publisher={{jalink|Kogakkan University|Q1797584}}Publishing Department|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Kuga Shrine (Shikiuchisha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor={{jalink|Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596}}edited|author=Masayoshi Genjo|year=1986|chapter=Kuga Shrine|title=Japanese Gods -Shrines and Sacred Sites- 5 Yamashiro/Omi|publisher={{jalink|Hakusuisha|Q4346470}}|isbn=4560022151|ref={{Harvid|Kuga Shrine (Gods)|1985}}}}* {{Cite book|Japanese book|author=Mitsugi Kennai|chapter=The history of Setsusesha|year=2003|title=Kamigamo Shrine|publisher={{jalink|Kamikamo Shrine|Q11448819}}|isbn=4311407203|ref={{Harvid|Kamigamo Shrine (Kamikazesha)|2003}}}} * “Kyoto Place Name Verification” (edited by Kyoto Place Name Research Group, Bensei Publishing, 2005) * “Exploring Kyoto, Kamigamo and the Two Kurama Highways – Past and Present” (Keiichiro Nishimura (individual), 2008) === Related items === {{Commonscat|Kuga-jinja}} * {{jalink|Kamobetsu Rai Shrine|Q700448}} (Kamigamo Shrine) * {{jalink|Omiya-dori|Q11434468}} (Shin-Omiya-dori) * {{jalink|Air Shrine|Q30319396}} {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kukashinshiya Kiyoto Shitoku}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuga
