{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=280|coord={{coord|35|3|7.4|N|135|44|52.7|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kyoto City|Q34600|WD=Q34600|1=Kyoto}}{{ill|lt=Kita Ward (Kyoto City)|Q1154970|WD=Q1154970|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Kyoto}}47 Shichiku Shimotakedonomachi |Location = {{Coord|35|3|7.4|N|135|44|52.7|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kuga Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Kamo Kenkaku no Mikoto|Q11634939|WD=Q11634939|1=Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto}} |Divinity = |Shaku = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
{{ill|lt=Kamobetsu Raijinja|Q700448|WD=Q700448|1=Kamigamo Shrine}}Kyotogaisetsusha |Foundation = unknown |Main shrine = Ikkensha {{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}{{ill|lt=cypress bark roof|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} |Alternative name = Uji Shrine |Fudasho, etc. =|Regular Festival = {{ill|lt=April 1st|Q2510|WD=Q2510|1=April 1}}・{{ill|lt=November 1st|Q2997|WD=Q2997|1=November 1}}{{Sfn|Kamigamo Shrine (Student Shrine)|2003|pp=51-52}} |Shinto ritual = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto city|label=Kuga Shrine|position=bottom|width=180|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} [[File:Kuga-jinja (Kyoto) nishi-torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nishi Torii]] '''Kuga Shrine'' (Kugajinja) is located in {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kyoto City|Q34600|WD=Q34600|1=Kyoto}}{{ill|lt=Kita Ward_(Kyoto City)|Q1154970|WD=Q1154970|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Kyoto}} Shichiku-Shimotakedono-cho.{{ill|lt=Shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}}. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinai Shrine|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}}, currently outside the precincts of {{ill|lt=Kamobetsu Rai Shrine|Q700448|WD=Q700448|1=Kamigamo Shrine}} (Kamigamo Shrine) {{ill|lt=Setsusha|Q135009904|WD=Q135009904|1=Sessha}} (8th Setsusha). The former name was ``''Uji Shrine''''. {{Sfn|Kamigamo Shrine (Student Shrine)|2003|pp=51-52}} is located about 1 km west of Kamigamo Shrine. === Enshrined God === The following deity is enshrined: {{Sfn|Kamigamo Shrine (Student Shrine)|2003|pp=51-52}}. * '''{{ill|lt=Kamo Kenkaku no Mikoto|Q11634939|WD=Q11634939|1=Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto}}''' (Kamotake Tsunumi Mikoto) *: Ancestor god of {{ill|lt=Kamo Prefectural Lord|Q2975287|WD=Q2975287|lt=Kamo Prefectural Lord|1=Kamo clan}} (Kamo clan). He was the grandfather of Kamigamo Shrine's enshrined deity, {{ill|lt=Kamobetsu Rai-no-Mikoto|Q11634943|WD=Q11634943|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} (Kamowakeikazuchi no Mikoto), {{jalink|Emperor Jimmu|Q2001 It is identified with {{ill|lt=Yatagarasu|Q745463|WD=Q745463|1=Yatagarasu}} (Yatagarasu) who led the {{ill|lt=Jinmu Tosei|Q11590444|WD=Q11590444|lt=Tosei|1=Jimmu's Eastern Expedition}} in 88}}. Since ancient times the company name has been called ``Uji Shrine'', the enshrined deity is said to be Kamo Takekaku no Mikoto, the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan, but according to Kamigamo Shrine documents, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Early modern|1=UNKNOWN}} also had different theories such as {{ill|lt=Kuni no Tokodatejin|Q478504|WD=Q478504|lt=Kuni no Tokodateson|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}} {{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1979}}. === History === {{List of coordinates}} === Founding === [[File:Koga-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Kuga Shrine (Kyoto City{{ill|lt=Fushimi Ward|Q1197771|WD=Q1197771|1=Fushimi-ku, Kyoto}})}}]] Founding date is unknown. Our company is believed to be one of the shrines {{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Gods)|1985}} that was enshrined by {{ill|lt=Kamo Prefectural Lord|Q2975287|WD=Q2975287|1=Kamo clan}} when he moved from {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato|1=Yamato Province}} to {{ill|lt=Yamashiro Province|Q749276|WD=Q749276|lt=Yamashiro|1=Yamashiro Province}}. "{{ill|lt=Fudoki|Q1194534|WD=Q1194534|lt=Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki|1=Fudoki}}" Isshu Isshu of "Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki" quoted in volume 9 of "Shaku Nihongi". According to , after Kamo Kenkakumi no Mikoto achieved great success in Emperor Jimmu's eastern expedition, he moved from Yamato to ``Kamo in Okada, Yamashiro Province'', ``Kazuno River ({{ill|lt=Katsura River (Yodo River system)|Q2909307|WD=Q2909307|lt=Katsura River|1=Katsura River}}) and Kamo River ({{jalink|Kamogawa River}). (Kyoto Prefecture) | Q27218 | Kamogawa}})" and "Yamamoto in the north of Kuga no Kuni", and the guardian deities of the Kamo clan are still enshrined at each location {{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Gods) | 1985}}. The specific locations for each location are as follows.# Kamo, Okada, Yamashiro no Kuni - present-day Kamo Town, Kyoto Prefecture {{ill|lt=Kizugawa City|Q990352|WD=Q990352|1=Kizugawa, Kyoto}}. The {{ill|lt=Okada Kamo Shrine|Q11473738|WD=Q11473738|1=Okada Kamo Shrine}} ({{Coord|34|45|41.55|N|135|52|34.92|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|name=1. Okada Kamo Shrine}}) of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinai Shrine|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} is enshrined here. # Where the Kazuno River meets the Kamo River - present-day Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture {{ill|lt=Fushimi Ward|Q1197771|WD=Q1197771|1=Fushimi-ku, Kyoto}}. The Shikinai shrine, Kuga Shrine (currently {{ill|lt=Kuga Shrine (Fushimi Ward, Kyoto City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Kuga Shrine}}, {{Coord|34|56|26.37|N|135|43|48.94|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|name=2. Kuga Shrine (currently Kuga Shrine)}}) is located there. # Yamamoto in the north of Kuga no Kuni - near our company. Regarding the place name "{{ill|lt=Yamase Kuga Kunizo|UNKNOWN|lt=Kuga (country)}}", there is a theory that it is an old name for the entire area, but it declined as the Kamo clan's influence spread, but it was originally the name of {{ill|lt=Otokuni District|Q376858|WD=Q376858|1=Otokuni District, Kyoto}}, where Kuga Shrine (Kuga Shrine) is located. There is a theory that the name was followed in this area, and another theory that the original name is {{ill|lt=Kii-gun|Q11604966|WD=Q11604966|1=Kii district}} of Minamiyama Castle, not Otokuni-gun. There is a tradition that our company was enshrined at its current location because there was a tomb of Kenkaku Kamo no Mikoto nearby, but this is not clear {{Sfn|Kamigamo Shrine (Student Shrine)|2003|pp=51-52}} === Outline === In {{ill|lt=Rokukokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=Kokushi|1=Rikkokushi}}, {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 1st year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}) ``Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' article on the 27th day of the first month of Jogan 1st year (859). In , it appears that the {{ill|lt=Shinkai|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} of ``Kugagami'' was promoted from the upper rank of upper sixth rank to lower rank of junior fifth rank {{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. Also, {{ill|lt=extension (Era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extended|1=Enchō}} 5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}" {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmyocho|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} link|Yamashiro Province|Q749276}}{{ill|lt=Atago District|Q10981345|WD=Q10981345|1=Otagi district}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi-sha|1=Shikinaisha}} is listed as "Kuga Shrine", and these shrines are compared to our company {{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Gods)|1985}}.Since our company is listed as a subsidiary shrine in Kamo Shrine's ``Kagen Annual Events'', it is assumed that it was listed as a subsidiary shrine of Kamo Shrine from around the late {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}{{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}. In addition, the company name was also called "Uji Shrine" after {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}. . However, in terms of rituals, by {{ill|lt=Middle Ages|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}}, the Kamo clan's character as a guardian deity had already faded, and its character as a shrine for the local people had strengthened {{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1979}}. In {{ill|lt=Horeki|Q1194397|WD=Q1194397|1=Hōreki}} 4th year ({{ill|lt=1754|Q7623|WD=Q7623|1=1754}}) "{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamashiro Famous Sites Pilgrimage|1=UNKNOWN}}" At the northern end of Shichiku, there is an old shrine called Omiya no Mori, which is called Haku no Mori, and you can see the description that {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} faces west and the hall of worship faces south. Since {{ill|lt=early modern times|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718|1=Early modern period}}, our company was mainly called "Uji Shrine" or "Omiya", but {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096} In }5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), the company name was changed to "Kuga Shrine" and remains the current name {{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. === God level === * {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} January 27, 1st year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), from upper 6th rank to lower rank of 5th rank (``{{ill|lt=Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - Written as ``Kugagami.'' === Precincts === [[File:Kuga-jinja2.JPG|thumb|200px|A zelkova stump
{{Smaller|The tree is said to be 600 years old.{{Sfn|Kamigamo Shrine (Student Shrine)|2003|pp=51-52}}. }}]] In the ``{{ill|lt=Yongshu Fushi|Q18460389|WD=Q18460389|1=Yongshu Fushi}}'' written in 1686|Q7664}}, the precincts are commonly referred to as the ``Omiya Forest,'' and there are still giant {{ill|lt=zelkova|Q920344|WD=Q920344|1=Zelkova serrata}} trees on the premises that still bear traces of that past.. The temple grounds have been designated as a Kyoto City designated historic site, as the tradition of the migration of the Kamo clan has been passed down to the present day, and the environment that reveals the history of Kyoto has been preserved in good condition. The current main shrine and worship hall were built in {{ill|lt=Kanei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}}5 ({{ill|lt=1628|Q6840|WD=Q6840|1=1628}}){{Sfn|Kamigamo Shrine (Student Shrine)|2003|pp=51-52}}. The main shrine is in the style of Ikken-sha {{ill|lt=Nagazuzukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}, with a {{ill|lt=cypress bark roof|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} roof, and is the same structure as other shrines of Kamigamo Shrine. The worship hall has a {{ill|lt=gabled structure|Q13308575|WD=Q13308575|1=Kirizuma-style}} gable with one beam and two beams, and the roof is also thatched with cypress bark.Furthermore, there is a legend that Kuga Shrine was called ``Omiya'' from the time of its establishment, and that the name of the area was ``Omiya'' (Omiya-go) and the street name in front of the gate was ``Omiya-dori.'' That street is {{ill|lt=Daitokuji-dori|Q11435442|WD=Q11435442|1=Daitokuji Street}}, which locals still refer to as ``Old Omiya-dori'' today. === Festival === * Spring Festival ({{ill|lt=April 1st|Q2510|WD=Q2510|1=April 1}}) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hassaku Festival|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=September 1st|Q2859|WD=Q2859|1=September 1}}) * Autumn Festival ({{ill|lt=November 1st|Q2997|WD=Q2997|1=November 1}}) * Shinko Festival ({{ill|lt=November 3rd|Q2995|WD=Q2995|1=November 3}}) - {{ill|lt=Mikoshi|Mikoshi|WD=Mikoshi|ltq=Q979940}} parades through the shrine parishioners of the four former Omiya-go school districts (Omiya, Taiho, Shichiku, and Motomachi). During the spring and autumn festivals, a ritual called ``Hikiuma-nogi'' is held in which Kamigamo Shrine's sacred horse circles the main shrine three times. === Cultural assets === === Kyoto City designated historic site === * Kuga Shrine Precincts - Designated on May 1, 1988 [https://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/bunshi/page/0000005881.html Kyoto City Designated/Registered Cultural Property - Historic Site - Kita Ward] (Kyoto City website "Kyoto City Information Center"). . === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} ;Original text {{Reflist|group="Original"}} === References === * Shrine history book * Precinct information board (installed in Kyoto City) * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Kuga Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Japanese historical place name system|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 27 Kyoto city place name|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harvid|Kuga Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=Shikiuchisha Research Group|author=Yasuo Izumiya|chapter=Kuga Shrine|year=1979|title=Shikiuchisha Investigation Report Volume 1|publisher={{ill|lt=Kogakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}}Publishing Department|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Kuga Shrine (Shikiuchisha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor={{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}}edited|author=Masayoshi Genjo|year=1986|chapter=Kuga Shrine|title=Japanese Gods -Shrines and Sacred Sites- 5 Yamashiro/Omi|publisher={{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}|isbn=4560022151|ref={{Harvid|Kuga Shrine (Gods)|1985}}}}* {{Cite book|Japanese book|author=Mitsugi Kennai|chapter=The history of Setsusesha|year=2003|title=Kamigamo Shrine|publisher={{ill|lt=Kamikamo Shrine|Q11448819|WD=Q11448819|1=Gakuseisha}}|isbn=4311407203|ref={{Harvid|Kamigamo Shrine (Kamikazesha)|2003}}}} * “Kyoto Place Name Verification” (edited by Kyoto Place Name Research Group, Bensei Publishing, 2005) * “Exploring Kyoto, Kamigamo and the Two Kurama Highways – Past and Present” (Keiichiro Nishimura (individual), 2008) === Related items === {{Commonscat|Kuga-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=Kamobetsu Rai Shrine|Q700448|WD=Q700448|1=Kamigamo Shrine}} (Kamigamo Shrine) * Shrine building
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=280|coord={{coord|35|3|7.4|N|135|44|52.7|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kyoto City|Q34600|WD=Q34600|1=Kyoto}}{{ill|lt=Kita Ward (Kyoto City)|Q1154970|WD=Q1154970|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Kyoto}}47 Shichiku Shimotakedonomachi |Location = {{Coord|35|3|7.4|N|135|44|52.7|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kuga Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Kamo Kenkaku no Mikoto|Q11634939|WD=Q11634939|1=Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto}} |Divinity = |Shaku = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
{{ill|lt=Kamobetsu Raijinja|Q700448|WD=Q700448|1=Kamigamo Shrine}}Kyotogaisetsusha |Foundation = unknown |Main shrine = Ikkensha {{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}{{ill|lt=cypress bark roof|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} |Alternative name = Uji Shrine |Fudasho, etc. =|Regular Festival = {{ill|lt=April 1st|Q2510|WD=Q2510|1=April 1}}・{{ill|lt=November 1st|Q2997|WD=Q2997|1=November 1}}{{Sfn|Kamigamo Shrine (Student Shrine)|2003|pp=51-52}} |Shinto ritual = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto city|label=Kuga Shrine|position=bottom|width=180|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} [[File:Kuga-jinja (Kyoto) nishi-torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nishi Torii]] '''Kuga Shrine'' (Kugajinja) is located in {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kyoto City|Q34600|WD=Q34600|1=Kyoto}}{{ill|lt=Kita Ward_(Kyoto City)|Q1154970|WD=Q1154970|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Kyoto}} Shichiku-Shimotakedono-cho.{{ill|lt=Shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}}. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinai Shrine|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}}, currently outside the precincts of {{ill|lt=Kamobetsu Rai Shrine|Q700448|WD=Q700448|1=Kamigamo Shrine}} (Kamigamo Shrine) {{ill|lt=Setsusha|Q135009904|WD=Q135009904|1=Sessha}} (8th Setsusha). The former name was ``''Uji Shrine''''. {{Sfn|Kamigamo Shrine (Student Shrine)|2003|pp=51-52}} is located about 1 km west of Kamigamo Shrine. === Enshrined God === The following deity is enshrined: {{Sfn|Kamigamo Shrine (Student Shrine)|2003|pp=51-52}}. * '''{{ill|lt=Kamo Kenkaku no Mikoto|Q11634939|WD=Q11634939|1=Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto}}''' (Kamotake Tsunumi Mikoto) *: Ancestor god of {{ill|lt=Kamo Prefectural Lord|Q2975287|WD=Q2975287|lt=Kamo Prefectural Lord|1=Kamo clan}} (Kamo clan). He was the grandfather of Kamigamo Shrine's enshrined deity, {{ill|lt=Kamobetsu Rai-no-Mikoto|Q11634943|WD=Q11634943|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} (Kamowakeikazuchi no Mikoto), {{jalink|Emperor Jimmu|Q2001 It is identified with {{ill|lt=Yatagarasu|Q745463|WD=Q745463|1=Yatagarasu}} (Yatagarasu) who led the {{ill|lt=Jinmu Tosei|Q11590444|WD=Q11590444|lt=Tosei|1=Jimmu's Eastern Expedition}} in 88}}. Since ancient times the company name has been called ``Uji Shrine'', the enshrined deity is said to be Kamo Takekaku no Mikoto, the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan, but according to Kamigamo Shrine documents, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Early modern|1=UNKNOWN}} also had different theories such as {{ill|lt=Kuni no Tokodatejin|Q478504|WD=Q478504|lt=Kuni no Tokodateson|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}} {{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1979}}. === History === {{List of coordinates}} === Founding === [[File:Koga-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Kuga Shrine (Kyoto City{{ill|lt=Fushimi Ward|Q1197771|WD=Q1197771|1=Fushimi-ku, Kyoto}})}}]] Founding date is unknown. Our company is believed to be one of the shrines {{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Gods)|1985}} that was enshrined by {{ill|lt=Kamo Prefectural Lord|Q2975287|WD=Q2975287|1=Kamo clan}} when he moved from {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato|1=Yamato Province}} to {{ill|lt=Yamashiro Province|Q749276|WD=Q749276|lt=Yamashiro|1=Yamashiro Province}}. "{{ill|lt=Fudoki|Q1194534|WD=Q1194534|lt=Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki|1=Fudoki}}" Isshu Isshu of "Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki" quoted in volume 9 of "Shaku Nihongi". According to , after Kamo Kenkakumi no Mikoto achieved great success in Emperor Jimmu's eastern expedition, he moved from Yamato to ``Kamo in Okada, Yamashiro Province'', ``Kazuno River ({{ill|lt=Katsura River (Yodo River system)|Q2909307|WD=Q2909307|lt=Katsura River|1=Katsura River}}) and Kamo River ({{jalink|Kamogawa River}). (Kyoto Prefecture) | Q27218 | Kamogawa}})" and "Yamamoto in the north of Kuga no Kuni", and the guardian deities of the Kamo clan are still enshrined at each location {{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Gods) | 1985}}. The specific locations for each location are as follows.# Kamo, Okada, Yamashiro no Kuni - present-day Kamo Town, Kyoto Prefecture {{ill|lt=Kizugawa City|Q990352|WD=Q990352|1=Kizugawa, Kyoto}}. The {{ill|lt=Okada Kamo Shrine|Q11473738|WD=Q11473738|1=Okada Kamo Shrine}} ({{Coord|34|45|41.55|N|135|52|34.92|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|name=1. Okada Kamo Shrine}}) of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinai Shrine|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} is enshrined here. # Where the Kazuno River meets the Kamo River - present-day Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture {{ill|lt=Fushimi Ward|Q1197771|WD=Q1197771|1=Fushimi-ku, Kyoto}}. The Shikinai shrine, Kuga Shrine (currently {{ill|lt=Kuga Shrine (Fushimi Ward, Kyoto City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Kuga Shrine}}, {{Coord|34|56|26.37|N|135|43|48.94|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|name=2. Kuga Shrine (currently Kuga Shrine)}}) is located there. # Yamamoto in the north of Kuga no Kuni - near our company. Regarding the place name "{{ill|lt=Yamase Kuga Kunizo|UNKNOWN|lt=Kuga (country)}}", there is a theory that it is an old name for the entire area, but it declined as the Kamo clan's influence spread, but it was originally the name of {{ill|lt=Otokuni District|Q376858|WD=Q376858|1=Otokuni District, Kyoto}}, where Kuga Shrine (Kuga Shrine) is located. There is a theory that the name was followed in this area, and another theory that the original name is {{ill|lt=Kii-gun|Q11604966|WD=Q11604966|1=Kii district}} of Minamiyama Castle, not Otokuni-gun. There is a tradition that our company was enshrined at its current location because there was a tomb of Kenkaku Kamo no Mikoto nearby, but this is not clear {{Sfn|Kamigamo Shrine (Student Shrine)|2003|pp=51-52}} === Outline === In {{ill|lt=Rokukokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=Kokushi|1=Rikkokushi}}, {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 1st year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}) ``Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' article on the 27th day of the first month of Jogan 1st year (859). In , it appears that the {{ill|lt=Shinkai|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} of ``Kugagami'' was promoted from the upper rank of upper sixth rank to lower rank of junior fifth rank {{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. Also, {{ill|lt=extension (Era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extended|1=Enchō}} 5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}" {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmyocho|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} link|Yamashiro Province|Q749276}}{{ill|lt=Atago District|Q10981345|WD=Q10981345|1=Otagi district}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi-sha|1=Shikinaisha}} is listed as "Kuga Shrine", and these shrines are compared to our company {{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Gods)|1985}}.Since our company is listed as a subsidiary shrine in Kamo Shrine's ``Kagen Annual Events'', it is assumed that it was listed as a subsidiary shrine of Kamo Shrine from around the late {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}{{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}. In addition, the company name was also called "Uji Shrine" after {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}. . However, in terms of rituals, by {{ill|lt=Middle Ages|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}}, the Kamo clan's character as a guardian deity had already faded, and its character as a shrine for the local people had strengthened {{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1979}}. In {{ill|lt=Horeki|Q1194397|WD=Q1194397|1=Hōreki}} 4th year ({{ill|lt=1754|Q7623|WD=Q7623|1=1754}}) "{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamashiro Famous Sites Pilgrimage|1=UNKNOWN}}" At the northern end of Shichiku, there is an old shrine called Omiya no Mori, which is called Haku no Mori, and you can see the description that {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} faces west and the hall of worship faces south. Since {{ill|lt=early modern times|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718|1=Early modern period}}, our company was mainly called "Uji Shrine" or "Omiya", but {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096} In }5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), the company name was changed to "Kuga Shrine" and remains the current name {{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. === God level === * {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} January 27, 1st year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), from upper 6th rank to lower rank of 5th rank (``{{ill|lt=Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - Written as ``Kugagami.'' === Precincts === [[File:Kuga-jinja2.JPG|thumb|200px|A zelkova stump
{{Smaller|The tree is said to be 600 years old.{{Sfn|Kamigamo Shrine (Student Shrine)|2003|pp=51-52}}. }}]] In the ``{{ill|lt=Yongshu Fushi|Q18460389|WD=Q18460389|1=Yongshu Fushi}}'' written in 1686|Q7664}}, the precincts are commonly referred to as the ``Omiya Forest,'' and there are still giant {{ill|lt=zelkova|Q920344|WD=Q920344|1=Zelkova serrata}} trees on the premises that still bear traces of that past.. The temple grounds have been designated as a Kyoto City designated historic site, as the tradition of the migration of the Kamo clan has been passed down to the present day, and the environment that reveals the history of Kyoto has been preserved in good condition. The current main shrine and worship hall were built in {{ill|lt=Kanei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}}5 ({{ill|lt=1628|Q6840|WD=Q6840|1=1628}}){{Sfn|Kamigamo Shrine (Student Shrine)|2003|pp=51-52}}. The main shrine is in the style of Ikken-sha {{ill|lt=Nagazuzukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}, with a {{ill|lt=cypress bark roof|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} roof, and is the same structure as other shrines of Kamigamo Shrine. The worship hall has a {{ill|lt=gabled structure|Q13308575|WD=Q13308575|1=Kirizuma-style}} gable with one beam and two beams, and the roof is also thatched with cypress bark.Furthermore, there is a legend that Kuga Shrine was called ``Omiya'' from the time of its establishment, and that the name of the area was ``Omiya'' (Omiya-go) and the street name in front of the gate was ``Omiya-dori.'' That street is {{ill|lt=Daitokuji-dori|Q11435442|WD=Q11435442|1=Daitokuji Street}}, which locals still refer to as ``Old Omiya-dori'' today. === Festival === * Spring Festival ({{ill|lt=April 1st|Q2510|WD=Q2510|1=April 1}}) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hassaku Festival|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=September 1st|Q2859|WD=Q2859|1=September 1}}) * Autumn Festival ({{ill|lt=November 1st|Q2997|WD=Q2997|1=November 1}}) * Shinko Festival ({{ill|lt=November 3rd|Q2995|WD=Q2995|1=November 3}}) - {{ill|lt=Mikoshi|Mikoshi|WD=Mikoshi|ltq=Q979940}} parades through the shrine parishioners of the four former Omiya-go school districts (Omiya, Taiho, Shichiku, and Motomachi). During the spring and autumn festivals, a ritual called ``Hikiuma-nogi'' is held in which Kamigamo Shrine's sacred horse circles the main shrine three times. === Cultural assets === === Kyoto City designated historic site === * Kuga Shrine Precincts - Designated on May 1, 1988 [https://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/bunshi/page/0000005881.html Kyoto City Designated/Registered Cultural Property - Historic Site - Kita Ward] (Kyoto City website "Kyoto City Information Center"). . === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} ;Original text {{Reflist|group="Original"}} === References === * Shrine history book * Precinct information board (installed in Kyoto City) * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Kuga Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Japanese historical place name system|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 27 Kyoto city place name|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harvid|Kuga Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=Shikiuchisha Research Group|author=Yasuo Izumiya|chapter=Kuga Shrine|year=1979|title=Shikiuchisha Investigation Report Volume 1|publisher={{ill|lt=Kogakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}}Publishing Department|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Kuga Shrine (Shikiuchisha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor={{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}}edited|author=Masayoshi Genjo|year=1986|chapter=Kuga Shrine|title=Japanese Gods -Shrines and Sacred Sites- 5 Yamashiro/Omi|publisher={{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}|isbn=4560022151|ref={{Harvid|Kuga Shrine (Gods)|1985}}}}* {{Cite book|Japanese book|author=Mitsugi (Kamikazesha)|2003}}}} * “Kyoto Place Name Verification” (edited by Kyoto Place Name Research Group, Bensei Publishing, 2005) * “Exploring Kyoto, Kamigamo and the Two Kurama Highways – Past and Present” (Keiichiro Nishimura (individual), 2008) === Related items === {{Commonscat|Kuga-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=Kamobetsu Rai Shrine|Q700448|WD=Q700448|1=Kamigamo Shrine}} (Kamigamo Shrine) * {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=280|coord={{coord|35|3|7.4|N|135|44|52.7|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|qid=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kyoto City|Q34600|qid=Q34600|1=Kyoto}}{{ill|lt=Kita Ward (Kyoto City)|Q1154970|qid=Q1154970|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Kyoto}}47 Shichiku Shimotakedonomachi |Location = {{Coord|35|3|7.4|N|135|44|52.7|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kuga Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Kamo Kenkaku no Mikoto|Q11634939|qid=Q11634939|1=Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto}} |Divinity = |Shaku = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (small)
{{ill|lt=Kamobetsu Raijinja|Q700448|qid=Q700448|1=Kamigamo Shrine}}Kyotogaisetsusha |Foundation = unknown |Main shrine = Ikkensha {{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}{{ill|lt=cypress bark roof|Q90373477|qid=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} |Alternative name = Uji Shrine |Fudasho, etc. =|Regular Festival = {{ill|lt=April 1st|Q2510|qid=Q2510|1=April 1}}・{{ill|lt=November 1st|Q2997|qid=Q2997|1=November 1}}{{Sfn|Kamigamo Shrine (Student Shrine)|2003|pp=51-52}} |Shinto ritual = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto city|label=Kuga Shrine|position=bottom|width=180|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} [[File:Kuga-jinja (Kyoto) nishi-torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nishi Torii]] '''Kuga Shrine'' (Kugajinja) is located in {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|qid=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kyoto City|Q34600|qid=Q34600|1=Kyoto}}{{ill|lt=Kita Ward_(Kyoto City)|Q1154970|qid=Q1154970|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Kyoto}} Shichiku-Shimotakedono-cho.{{ill|lt=Shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}}. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinai Shrine|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}}, currently outside the precincts of {{ill|lt=Kamobetsu Rai Shrine|Q700448|qid=Q700448|1=Kamigamo Shrine}} (Kamigamo Shrine) {{ill|lt=Setsusha|Q135009904|qid=Q135009904|1=Sessha}} (8th Setsusha). The former name was ``''Uji Shrine''''. {{Sfn|Kamigamo Shrine (Student Shrine)|2003|pp=51-52}} is located about 1 km west of Kamigamo Shrine. === Enshrined God === The following deity is enshrined: {{Sfn|Kamigamo Shrine (Student Shrine)|2003|pp=51-52}}. * '''{{ill|lt=Kamo Kenkaku no Mikoto|Q11634939|qid=Q11634939|1=Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto}}''' (Kamotake Tsunumi Mikoto) *: Ancestor god of {{ill|lt=Kamo Prefectural Lord|Q2975287|qid=Q2975287|lt=Kamo Prefectural Lord|1=Kamo clan}} (Kamo clan). He was the grandfather of Kamigamo Shrine's enshrined deity, {{ill|lt=Kamobetsu Rai-no-Mikoto|Q11634943|qid=Q11634943|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} (Kamowakeikazuchi no Mikoto), {{jalink|Emperor Jimmu|Q2001 It is identified with {{ill|lt=Yatagarasu|Q745463|qid=Q745463|1=Yatagarasu}} (Yatagarasu) who led the {{ill|lt=Jinmu Tosei|Q11590444|qid=Q11590444|lt=Tosei|1=Jimmu's Eastern Expedition}} in 88}}. Since ancient times the company name has been called ``Uji Shrine'', the enshrined deity is said to be Kamo Takekaku no Mikoto, the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan, but according to Kamigamo Shrine documents, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Early modern|1=UNKNOWN}} also had different theories such as {{ill|lt=Kuni no Tokodatejin|Q478504|qid=Q478504|lt=Kuni no Tokodateson|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}} {{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1979}}. === History === {{List of coordinates}} === Founding === [[File:Koga-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Kuga Shrine (Kyoto City{{ill|lt=Fushimi Ward|Q1197771|qid=Q1197771|1=Fushimi-ku, Kyoto}})}}]] Founding date is unknown. Our company is believed to be one of the shrines {{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Gods)|1985}} that was enshrined by {{ill|lt=Kamo Prefectural Lord|Q2975287|qid=Q2975287|1=Kamo clan}} when he moved from {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|qid=Q907495|lt=Yamato|1=Yamato Province}} to {{ill|lt=Yamashiro Province|Q749276|qid=Q749276|lt=Yamashiro|1=Yamashiro Province}}. "{{ill|lt=Fudoki|Q1194534|qid=Q1194534|lt=Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki|1=Fudoki}}" Isshu Isshu of "Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki" quoted in volume 9 of "Shaku Nihongi". According to , after Kamo Kenkakumi no Mikoto achieved great success in Emperor Jimmu's eastern expedition, he moved from Yamato to ``Kamo in Okada, Yamashiro Province'', ``Kazuno River ({{ill|lt=Katsura River (Yodo River system)|Q2909307|qid=Q2909307|lt=Katsura River|1=Katsura River}}) and Kamo River ({{jalink|Kamogawa River}). (Kyoto Prefecture) | Q27218 | Kamogawa}})" and "Yamamoto in the north of Kuga no Kuni", and the guardian deities of the Kamo clan are still enshrined at each location {{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Gods) | 1985}}. The specific locations for each location are as follows.# Kamo, Okada, Yamashiro no Kuni - present-day Kamo Town, Kyoto Prefecture {{ill|lt=Kizugawa City|Q990352|qid=Q990352|1=Kizugawa, Kyoto}}. The {{ill|lt=Okada Kamo Shrine|Q11473738|qid=Q11473738|1=Okada Kamo Shrine}} ({{Coord|34|45|41.55|N|135|52|34.92|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|name=1. Okada Kamo Shrine}}) of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinai Shrine|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} is enshrined here. # Where the Kazuno River meets the Kamo River - present-day Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture {{ill|lt=Fushimi Ward|Q1197771|qid=Q1197771|1=Fushimi-ku, Kyoto}}. The Shikinai shrine, Kuga Shrine (currently {{ill|lt=Kuga Shrine (Fushimi Ward, Kyoto City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Kuga Shrine}}, {{Coord|34|56|26.37|N|135|43|48.94|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|name=2. Kuga Shrine (currently Kuga Shrine)}}) is located there. # Yamamoto in the north of Kuga no Kuni - near our company. Regarding the place name "{{ill|lt=Yamase Kuga Kunizo|UNKNOWN|lt=Kuga (country)}}", there is a theory that it is an old name for the entire area, but it declined as the Kamo clan's influence spread, but it was originally the name of {{ill|lt=Otokuni District|Q376858|qid=Q376858|1=Otokuni District, Kyoto}}, where Kuga Shrine (Kuga Shrine) is located. There is a theory that the name was followed in this area, and another theory that the original name is {{ill|lt=Kii-gun|Q11604966|qid=Q11604966|1=Kii district}} of Minamiyama Castle, not Otokuni-gun. There is a tradition that our company was enshrined at its current location because there was a tomb of Kenkaku Kamo no Mikoto nearby, but this is not clear {{Sfn|Kamigamo Shrine (Student Shrine)|2003|pp=51-52}} === Outline === In {{ill|lt=Rokukokushi|Q1151168|qid=Q1151168|lt=Kokushi|1=Rikkokushi}}, {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 1st year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|qid=Q23897|1=859}}) ``Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' article on the 27th day of the first month of Jogan 1st year (859). In , it appears that the {{ill|lt=Shinkai|Q11591025|qid=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} of ``Kugagami'' was promoted from the upper rank of upper sixth rank to lower rank of junior fifth rank {{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. Also, {{ill|lt=extension (Era name)|Q1327274|qid=Q1327274|lt=Extended|1=Enchō}} 5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|qid=Q30470|1=927}}) "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}" {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Shinmyocho|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} link|Yamashiro Province|Q749276}}{{ill|lt=Atago District|Q10981345|qid=Q10981345|1=Otagi district}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi-sha|1=Shikinaisha}} is listed as "Kuga Shrine", and these shrines are compared to our company {{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Gods)|1985}}.Since our company is listed as a subsidiary shrine in Kamo Shrine's ``Kagen Annual Events'', it is assumed that it was listed as a subsidiary shrine of Kamo Shrine from around the late {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|qid=Q193292|1=Heian period}}{{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}. In addition, the company name was also called "Uji Shrine" after {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|qid=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}. . However, in terms of rituals, by {{ill|lt=Middle Ages|Q12554|qid=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}}, the Kamo clan's character as a guardian deity had already faded, and its character as a shrine for the local people had strengthened {{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1979}}. In {{ill|lt=Horeki|Q1194397|qid=Q1194397|1=Hōreki}} 4th year ({{ill|lt=1754|Q7623|qid=Q7623|1=1754}}) "{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamashiro Famous Sites Pilgrimage|1=UNKNOWN}}" At the northern end of Shichiku, there is an old shrine called Omiya no Mori, which is called Haku no Mori, and you can see the description that {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|qid=Q234731|1=Torii}} faces west and the hall of worship faces south. Since {{ill|lt=early modern times|Q5308718|qid=Q5308718|1=Early modern period}}, our company was mainly called "Uji Shrine" or "Omiya", but {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096} In }5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|qid=Q7753|1=1872}}), the company name was changed to "Kuga Shrine" and remains the current name {{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. === God level === * {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} January 27, 1st year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|qid=Q23897|1=859}}), from upper 6th rank to lower rank of 5th rank (``{{ill|lt=Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|qid=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - Written as ``Kugagami.'' === Precincts === [[File:Kuga-jinja2.JPG|thumb|200px|A zelkova stump
{{Smaller|The tree is said to be 600 years old.{{Sfn|Kamigamo Shrine (Student Shrine)|2003|pp=51-52}}. }}]] In the ``{{ill|lt=Yongshu Fushi|Q18460389|qid=Q18460389|1=Yongshu Fushi}}'' written in 1686|Q7664}}, the precincts are commonly referred to as the ``Omiya Forest,'' and there are still giant {{ill|lt=zelkova|Q920344|qid=Q920344|1=Zelkova serrata}} trees on the premises that still bear traces of that past.. The temple grounds have been designated as a Kyoto City designated historic site, as the tradition of the migration of the Kamo clan has been passed down to the present day, and the environment that reveals the history of Kyoto has been preserved in good condition. The current main shrine and worship hall were built in {{ill|lt=Kanei|Q1192913|qid=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}}5 ({{ill|lt=1628|Q6840|qid=Q6840|1=1628}}){{Sfn|Kamigamo Shrine (Student Shrine)|2003|pp=51-52}}. The main shrine is in the style of Ikken-sha {{ill|lt=Nagazuzukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}, with a {{ill|lt=cypress bark roof|Q90373477|qid=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} roof, and is the same structure as other shrines of Kamigamo Shrine. The worship hall has a {{ill|lt=gabled structure|Q13308575|qid=Q13308575|1=Kirizuma-style}} gable with one beam and two beams, and the roof is also thatched with cypress bark.Furthermore, there is a legend that Kuga Shrine was called ``Omiya'' from the time of its establishment, and that the name of the area was ``Omiya'' (Omiya-go) and the street name in front of the gate was ``Omiya-dori.'' That street is {{ill|lt=Daitokuji-dori|Q11435442|qid=Q11435442|1=Daitokuji Street}}, which locals still refer to as ``Old Omiya-dori'' today. === Festival === * Spring Festival ({{ill|lt=April 1st|Q2510|qid=Q2510|1=April 1}}) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hassaku Festival|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=September 1st|Q2859|qid=Q2859|1=September 1}}) * Autumn Festival ({{ill|lt=November 1st|Q2997|qid=Q2997|1=November 1}}) * Shinko Festival ({{ill|lt=November 3rd|Q2995|qid=Q2995|1=November 3}}) - {{ill|lt=Mikoshi|Mikoshi|qid=Mikoshi|ltq=Q979940}} parades through the shrine parishioners of the four former Omiya-go school districts (Omiya, Taiho, Shichiku, and Motomachi). During the spring and autumn festivals, a ritual called ``Hikiuma-nogi'' is held in which Kamigamo Shrine's sacred horse circles the main shrine three times. === Cultural assets === === Kyoto City designated historic site === * Kuga Shrine Precincts - Designated on May 1, 1988 [https://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/bunshi/page/0000005881.html Kyoto City Designated/Registered Cultural Property - Historic Site - Kita Ward] (Kyoto City website "Kyoto City Information Center"). . === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} ;Original text {{Reflist|group="Original"}} === References === * Shrine history book * Precinct information board (installed in Kyoto City) * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Kuga Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Japanese historical place name system|Q17213509|qid=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 27 Kyoto city place name|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|qid=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harvid|Kuga Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=Shikiuchisha Research Group|author=Yasuo Izumiya|chapter=Kuga Shrine|year=1979|title=Shikiuchisha Investigation Report Volume 1|publisher={{ill|lt=Kogakkan University|Q1797584|qid=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}}Publishing Department|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Kuga Shrine (Shikiuchisha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor={{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|qid=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}}edited|author=Masayoshi Genjo|year=1986|chapter=Kuga Shrine|title=Japanese Gods -Shrines and Sacred Sites- 5 (Kamikazesha)|2003}}}} * “Kyoto Place Name Verification” (edited by Kyoto Place Name Research Group, Bensei Publishing, 2005) * “Exploring Kyoto, Kamigamo and the Two Kurama Highways – Past and {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=280|coord={{coord|35|3|7.4|N|135|44|52.7|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|qid=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kyoto City|Q34600|qid=Q34600|1=Kyoto}}{{ill|lt=Kita Ward (Kyoto City)|Q1154970|qid=Q1154970|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Kyoto}}47 Shichiku Shimotakedonomachi |Location = {{Coord|35|3|7.4|N|135|44|52.7|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kuga Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Kamo Kenkaku no Mikoto|Q11634939|qid=Q11634939|1=Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto}} |Divinity = |Shaku = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (small)
{{ill|lt=Kamobetsu Raijinja|Q700448|qid=Q700448|1=Kamigamo Shrine}}Kyotogaisetsusha |Foundation = unknown |Main shrine = Ikkensha {{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}{{ill|lt=cypress bark roof|Q90373477|qid=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} |Alternative name = Uji Shrine |Fudasho, etc. =|Regular Festival = {{ill|lt=April 1st|Q2510|qid=Q2510|1=April 1}}・{{ill|lt=November 1st|Q2997|qid=Q2997|1=November 1}}{{Sfn|Kamigamo Shrine (Student Shrine)|2003|pp=51-52}} |Shinto ritual = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto city|label=Kuga Shrine|position=bottom|width=180|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} [[File:Kuga-jinja (Kyoto) nishi-torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nishi Torii]] '''Kuga Shrine'' (Kugajinja) is located in {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|qid=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kyoto City|Q34600|qid=Q34600|1=Kyoto}}{{ill|lt=Kita Ward_(Kyoto City)|Q1154970|qid=Q1154970|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Kyoto}} Shichiku-Shimotakedono-cho.{{ill|lt=Shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}}. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinai Shrine|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}}, currently outside the precincts of {{ill|lt=Kamobetsu Rai Shrine|Q700448|qid=Q700448|1=Kamigamo Shrine}} (Kamigamo Shrine) {{ill|lt=Setsusha|Q135009904|qid=Q135009904|1=Sessha}} (8th Setsusha). The former name was ``''Uji Shrine''''. {{Sfn|Kamigamo Shrine (Student Shrine)|2003|pp=51-52}} is located about 1 km west of Kamigamo Shrine. === Enshrined God === The following deity is enshrined: {{Sfn|Kamigamo Shrine (Student Shrine)|2003|pp=51-52}}. * '''{{ill|lt=Kamo Kenkaku no Mikoto|Q11634939|qid=Q11634939|1=Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto}}''' (Kamotake Tsunumi Mikoto) *: Ancestor god of {{ill|lt=Kamo Prefectural Lord|Q2975287|qid=Q2975287|lt=Kamo Prefectural Lord|1=Kamo clan}} (Kamo clan). He was the grandfather of Kamigamo Shrine's enshrined deity, {{ill|lt=Kamobetsu Rai-no-Mikoto|Q11634943|qid=Q11634943|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} (Kamowakeikazuchi no Mikoto), {{jalink|Emperor Jimmu|Q2001 It is identified with {{ill|lt=Yatagarasu|Q745463|qid=Q745463|1=Yatagarasu}} (Yatagarasu) who led the {{ill|lt=Jinmu Tosei|Q11590444|qid=Q11590444|lt=Tosei|1=Jimmu's Eastern Expedition}} in 88}}. Since ancient times the company name has been called ``Uji Shrine'', the enshrined deity is said to be Kamo Takekaku no Mikoto, the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan, but according to Kamigamo Shrine documents, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Early modern|1=UNKNOWN}} also had different theories such as {{ill|lt=Kuni no Tokodatejin|Q478504|qid=Q478504|lt=Kuni no Tokodateson|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}} {{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1979}}. === History === {{List of coordinates}} === Founding === [[File:Koga-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Kuga Shrine (Kyoto City{{ill|lt=Fushimi Ward|Q1197771|qid=Q1197771|1=Fushimi-ku, Kyoto}})}}]] Founding date is unknown. Our company is believed to be one of the shrines {{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Gods)|1985}} that was enshrined by {{ill|lt=Kamo Prefectural Lord|Q2975287|qid=Q2975287|1=Kamo clan}} when he moved from {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|qid=Q907495|lt=Yamato|1=Yamato Province}} to {{ill|lt=Yamashiro Province|Q749276|qid=Q749276|lt=Yamashiro|1=Yamashiro Province}}. "{{ill|lt=Fudoki|Q1194534|qid=Q1194534|lt=Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki|1=Fudoki}}" Isshu Isshu of "Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki" quoted in volume 9 of "Shaku Nihongi". According to , after Kamo Kenkakumi no Mikoto achieved great success in Emperor Jimmu's eastern expedition, he moved from Yamato to ``Kamo in Okada, Yamashiro Province'', ``Kazuno River ({{ill|lt=Katsura River (Yodo River system)|Q2909307|qid=Q2909307|lt=Katsura River|1=Katsura River}}) and Kamo River ({{jalink|Kamogawa River}). (Kyoto Prefecture) | Q27218 | Kamogawa}})" and "Yamamoto in the north of Kuga no Kuni", and the guardian deities of the Kamo clan are still enshrined at each location {{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Gods) | 1985}}. The specific locations for each location are as follows.# Kamo, Okada, Yamashiro no Kuni - present-day Kamo Town, Kyoto Prefecture {{ill|lt=Kizugawa City|Q990352|qid=Q990352|1=Kizugawa, Kyoto}}. The {{ill|lt=Okada Kamo Shrine|Q11473738|qid=Q11473738|1=Okada Kamo Shrine}} ({{Coord|34|45|41.55|N|135|52|34.92|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|name=1. Okada Kamo Shrine}}) of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinai Shrine|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} is enshrined here. # Where the Kazuno River meets the Kamo River - present-day Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture {{ill|lt=Fushimi Ward|Q1197771|qid=Q1197771|1=Fushimi-ku, Kyoto}}. The Shikinai shrine, Kuga Shrine (currently {{ill|lt=Kuga Shrine (Fushimi Ward, Kyoto City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Kuga Shrine}}, {{Coord|34|56|26.37|N|135|43|48.94|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|name=2. Kuga Shrine (currently Kuga Shrine)}}) is located there. # Yamamoto in the north of Kuga no Kuni - near our company. Regarding the place name "{{ill|lt=Yamase Kuga Kunizo|UNKNOWN|lt=Kuga (country)}}", there is a theory that it is an old name for the entire area, but it declined as the Kamo clan's influence spread, but it was originally the name of {{ill|lt=Otokuni District|Q376858|qid=Q376858|1=Otokuni District, Kyoto}}, where Kuga Shrine (Kuga Shrine) is located. There is a theory that the name was followed in this area, and another theory that the original name is {{ill|lt=Kii-gun|Q11604966|qid=Q11604966|1=Kii district}} of Minamiyama Castle, not Otokuni-gun. There is a tradition that our company was enshrined at its current location because there was a tomb of Kenkaku Kamo no Mikoto nearby, but this is not clear {{Sfn|Kamigamo Shrine (Student Shrine)|2003|pp=51-52}} === Outline === In {{ill|lt=Rokukokushi|Q1151168|qid=Q1151168|lt=Kokushi|1=Rikkokushi}}, {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 1st year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|qid=Q23897|1=859}}) ``Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' article on the 27th day of the first month of Jogan 1st year (859). In , it appears that the {{ill|lt=Shinkai|Q11591025|qid=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} of ``Kugagami'' was promoted from the upper rank of upper sixth rank to lower rank of junior fifth rank {{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. Also, {{ill|lt=extension (Era name)|Q1327274|qid=Q1327274|lt=Extended|1=Enchō}} 5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|qid=Q30470|1=927}}) "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}" {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Shinmyocho|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} link|Yamashiro Province|Q749276}}{{ill|lt=Atago District|Q10981345|qid=Q10981345|1=Otagi district}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi-sha|1=Shikinaisha}} is listed as "Kuga Shrine", and these shrines are compared to our company {{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Gods)|1985}}.Since our company is listed as a subsidiary shrine in Kamo Shrine's ``Kagen Annual Events'', it is assumed that it was listed as a subsidiary shrine of Kamo Shrine from around the late {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|qid=Q193292|1=Heian period}}{{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}. In addition, the company name was also called "Uji Shrine" after {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|qid=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}. . However, in terms of rituals, by {{ill|lt=Middle Ages|Q12554|qid=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}}, the Kamo clan's character as a guardian deity had already faded, and its character as a shrine for the local people had strengthened {{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1979}}. In {{ill|lt=Horeki|Q1194397|qid=Q1194397|1=Hōreki}} 4th year ({{ill|lt=1754|Q7623|qid=Q7623|1=1754}}) "{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamashiro Famous Sites Pilgrimage|1=UNKNOWN}}" At the northern end of Shichiku, there is an old shrine called Omiya no Mori, which is called Haku no Mori, and you can see the description that {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|qid=Q234731|1=Torii}} faces west and the hall of worship faces south. Since {{ill|lt=early modern times|Q5308718|qid=Q5308718|1=Early modern period}}, our company was mainly called "Uji Shrine" or "Omiya", but {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096} In }5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|qid=Q7753|1=1872}}), the company name was changed to "Kuga Shrine" and remains the current name {{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. === God level === * {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} January 27, 1st year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|qid=Q23897|1=859}}), from upper 6th rank to lower rank of 5th rank (``{{ill|lt=Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|qid=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - Written as ``Kugagami.'' === Precincts === [[File:Kuga-jinja2.JPG|thumb|200px|A zelkova stump
{{Smaller|The tree is said to be 600 years old.{{Sfn|Kamigamo Shrine (Student Shrine)|2003|pp=51-52}}. }}]] In the ``{{ill|lt=Yongshu Fushi|Q18460389|qid=Q18460389|1=Yongshu Fushi}}'' written in 1686|Q7664}}, the precincts are commonly referred to as the ``Omiya Forest,'' and there are still giant {{ill|lt=zelkova|Q920344|qid=Q920344|1=Zelkova serrata}} trees on the premises that still bear traces of that past.. The temple grounds have been designated as a Kyoto City designated historic site, as the tradition of the migration of the Kamo clan has been passed down to the present day, and the environment that reveals the history of Kyoto has been preserved in good condition. The current main shrine and worship hall were built in {{ill|lt=Kanei|Q1192913|qid=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}}5 ({{ill|lt=1628|Q6840|qid=Q6840|1=1628}}){{Sfn|Kamigamo Shrine (Student Shrine)|2003|pp=51-52}}. The main shrine is in the style of Ikken-sha {{ill|lt=Nagazuzukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}, with a {{ill|lt=cypress bark roof|Q90373477|qid=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} roof, and is the same structure as other shrines of Kamigamo Shrine. The worship hall has a {{ill|lt=gabled structure|Q13308575|qid=Q13308575|1=Kirizuma-style}} gable with one beam and two beams, and the roof is also thatched with cypress bark.Furthermore, there is a legend that Kuga Shrine was called ``Omiya'' from the time of its establishment, and that the name of the area was ``Omiya'' (Omiya-go) and the street name in front of the gate was ``Omiya-dori.'' That street is {{ill|lt=Daitokuji-dori|Q11435442|qid=Q11435442|1=Daitokuji Street}}, which locals still refer to as ``Old Omiya-dori'' today. === Festival === * Spring Festival ({{ill|lt=April 1st|Q2510|qid=Q2510|1=April 1}}) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hassaku Festival|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=September 1st|Q2859|qid=Q2859|1=September 1}}) * Autumn Festival ({{ill|lt=November 1st|Q2997|qid=Q2997|1=November 1}}) * Shinko Festival ({{ill|lt=November 3rd|Q2995|qid=Q2995|1=November 3}}) - {{ill|lt=Mikoshi|Mikoshi|qid=Mikoshi|ltq=Q979940}} parades through the shrine parishioners of the four former Omiya-go school districts (Omiya, Taiho, Shichiku, and Motomachi). During the spring and autumn festivals, a ritual called ``Hikiuma-nogi'' is held in which Kamigamo Shrine's sacred horse circles the main shrine three times. === Cultural assets === === Kyoto City designated historic site === * Kuga Shrine Precincts - Designated on May 1, 1988 [https://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/bunshi/page/0000005881.html Kyoto City Designated/Registered Cultural Property - Historic Site - Kita Ward] (Kyoto City website "Kyoto City Information Center"). . === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} ;Original text {{Reflist|group="Original"}} === References === * Shrine history book * Precinct information board (installed in Kyoto City) * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Kuga Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Japanese historical place name system|Q17213509|qid=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 27 Kyoto city place name|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|qid=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harvid|Kuga Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=Shikiuchisha Research Group|author=Yasuo Izumiya|chapter=Kuga Shrine|year=1979|title=Shikiuchisha Investigation Report Volume 1|publisher={{ill|lt=Kogakkan University|Q1797584|qid=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}}Publishing Department|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Kuga Shrine (Shikiuchisha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor={{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|qid=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}}edited|author=Masayoshi Genjo|year=1986|chapter=Kuga Shrine|title=Japanese Gods -Shrines and Sacred Sites- 5 (Kamikazesha)|2003}}}} * “Kyoto Place Name Verification” (edited by Kyoto Place Name Research Group, Bensei Publishing, 2005) * “Exploring Kyoto, Kamigamo and the Two Kurama Highways – Past and Present” (Keiichiro Nishimura (individual), 2008) === Related items === {{Commonscat|Kuga-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=Kamobetsu Rai Shrine|Q700448|qid=Q700448|1=Kamigamo Shrine}} (Kamigamo Shrine) * {{ill|lt=Kamobetsu Raijinja|Q700448|qid=Q700448|1=Kamigamo Shrine}}Kyotogaisetsusha |Foundation = unknown |Main shrine = Ikkensha {{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}{{ill|lt=cypress bark roof|Q90373477|qid=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} |Alternative name = Uji Shrine |Fudasho, etc. =|Regular Festival = {{ill|lt=April 1st|Q2510|qid=Q2510|1=April 1}}・{{ill|lt=November 1st|Q2997|qid=Q2997|1=November 1}}{{Sfn|Kamigamo Shrine (Student Shrine)|2003|pp=51-52}} |Shinto ritual = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto city|label=Kuga Shrine|position=bottom|width=180|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} [[File:Kuga-jinja (Kyoto) nishi-torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nishi Torii]] '''Kuga Shrine'' (Kugajinja) is located in {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|qid=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kyoto City|Q34600|qid=Q34600|1=Kyoto}}{{ill|lt=Kita Ward_(Kyoto City)|Q1154970|qid=Q1154970|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Kyoto}} Shichiku-Shimotakedono-cho.{{ill|lt=Shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}}. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinai Shrine|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}}, currently outside the precincts of {{ill|lt=Kamobetsu Rai Shrine|Q700448|qid=Q700448|1=Kamigamo Shrine}} (Kamigamo Shrine) {{ill|lt=Setsusha|Q135009904|qid=Q135009904|1=Sessha}} (8th Setsusha). The former name was ``''Uji Shrine''''. {{Sfn|Kamigamo Shrine (Student Shrine)|2003|pp=51-52}} is located about 1 km west of Kamigamo Shrine. === Enshrined God === The following deity is enshrined: {{Sfn|Kamigamo Shrine (Student Shrine)|2003|pp=51-52}}. * '''{{ill|lt=Kamo Kenkaku no Mikoto|Q11634939|qid=Q11634939|1=Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto}}''' (Kamotake Tsunumi Mikoto) *: Ancestor god of {{ill|lt=Kamo Prefectural Lord|Q2975287|qid=Q2975287|lt=Kamo Prefectural Lord|1=Kamo clan}} (Kamo clan). He was the grandfather of Kamigamo Shrine's enshrined deity, {{ill|lt=Kamobetsu Rai-no-Mikoto|Q11634943|qid=Q11634943|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} (Kamowakeikazuchi no Mikoto), {{jalink|Emperor Jimmu|Q2001 It is identified with {{ill|lt=Yatagarasu|Q745463|qid=Q745463|1=Yatagarasu}} (Yatagarasu) who led the {{ill|lt=Jinmu Tosei|Q11590444|qid=Q11590444|lt=Tosei|1=Jimmu's Eastern Expedition}} in 88}}. Since ancient times the company name has been called ``Uji Shrine'', the enshrined deity is said to be Kamo Takekaku no Mikoto, the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan, but according to Kamigamo Shrine documents, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Early modern|1=UNKNOWN}} also had different theories such as {{ill|lt=Kuni no Tokodatejin|Q478504|qid=Q478504|lt=Kuni no Tokodateson|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}} {{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1979}}. === History === {{List of coordinates}} === Founding === [[File:Koga-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Kuga Shrine (Kyoto City{{ill|lt=Fushimi Ward|Q1197771|qid=Q1197771|1=Fushimi-ku, Kyoto}})}}]] Founding date is unknown. Our company is believed to be one of the shrines {{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Gods)|1985}} that was enshrined by {{ill|lt=Kamo Prefectural Lord|Q2975287|qid=Q2975287|1=Kamo clan}} when he moved from {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|qid=Q907495|lt=Yamato|1=Yamato Province}} to {{ill|lt=Yamashiro Province|Q749276|qid=Q749276|lt=Yamashiro|1=Yamashiro Province}}. "{{ill|lt=Fudoki|Q1194534|qid=Q1194534|lt=Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki|1=Fudoki}}" Isshu Isshu of "Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki" quoted in volume 9 of "Shaku Nihongi". According to , after Kamo Kenkakumi no Mikoto achieved great success in Emperor Jimmu's eastern expedition, he moved from Yamato to ``Kamo in Okada, Yamashiro Province'', ``Kazuno River ({{ill|lt=Katsura River (Yodo River system)|Q2909307|qid=Q2909307|lt=Katsura River|1=Katsura River}}) and Kamo River ({{jalink|Kamogawa River}). (Kyoto Prefecture) | Q27218 | Kamogawa}})" and "Yamamoto in the north of Kuga no Kuni", and the guardian deities of the Kamo clan are still enshrined at each location {{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Gods) | 1985}}. The specific locations for each location are as follows.# Kamo, Okada, Yamashiro no Kuni - present-day Kamo Town, Kyoto Prefecture {{ill|lt=Kizugawa City|Q990352|qid=Q990352|1=Kizugawa, Kyoto}}. The {{ill|lt=Okada Kamo Shrine|Q11473738|qid=Q11473738|1=Okada Kamo Shrine}} ({{Coord|34|45|41.55|N|135|52|34.92|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|name=1. Okada Kamo Shrine}}) of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinai Shrine|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} is enshrined here. # Where the Kazuno River meets the Kamo River - present-day Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture {{ill|lt=Fushimi Ward|Q1197771|qid=Q1197771|1=Fushimi-ku, Kyoto}}. The Shikinai shrine, Kuga Shrine (currently {{ill|lt=Kuga Shrine (Fushimi Ward, Kyoto City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Kuga Shrine}}, {{Coord|34|56|26.37|N|135|43|48.94|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|name=2. Kuga Shrine (currently Kuga Shrine)}}) is located there. # Yamamoto in the north of Kuga no Kuni - near our company. Regarding the place name "{{ill|lt=Yamase Kuga Kunizo|UNKNOWN|lt=Kuga (country)}}", there is a theory that it is an old name for the entire area, but it declined as the Kamo clan's influence spread, but it was originally the name of {{ill|lt=Otokuni District|Q376858|qid=Q376858|1=Otokuni District, Kyoto}}, where Kuga Shrine (Kuga Shrine) is located. There is a theory that the name was followed in this area, and another theory that the original name is {{ill|lt=Kii-gun|Q11604966|qid=Q11604966|1=Kii district}} of Minamiyama Castle, not Otokuni-gun. There is a tradition that our company was enshrined at its current location because there was a tomb of Kenkaku Kamo no Mikoto nearby, but this is not clear {{Sfn|Kamigamo Shrine (Student Shrine)|2003|pp=51-52}} === Outline === In {{ill|lt=Rokukokushi|Q1151168|qid=Q1151168|lt=Kokushi|1=Rikkokushi}}, {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 1st year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|qid=Q23897|1=859}}) ``Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' article on the 27th day of the first month of Jogan 1st year (859). In , it appears that the {{ill|lt=Shinkai|Q11591025|qid=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} of ``Kugagami'' was promoted from the upper rank of upper sixth rank to lower rank of junior fifth rank {{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. Also, {{ill|lt=extension (Era name)|Q1327274|qid=Q1327274|lt=Extended|1=Enchō}} 5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|qid=Q30470|1=927}}) "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}" {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Shinmyocho|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} link|Yamashiro Province|Q749276}}{{ill|lt=Atago District|Q10981345|qid=Q10981345|1=Otagi district}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi-sha|1=Shikinaisha}} is listed as "Kuga Shrine", and these shrines are compared to our company {{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Gods)|1985}}.Since our company is listed as a subsidiary shrine in Kamo Shrine's ``Kagen Annual Events'', it is assumed that it was listed as a subsidiary shrine of Kamo Shrine from around the late {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|qid=Q193292|1=Heian period}}{{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}. In addition, the company name was also called "Uji Shrine" after {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|qid=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}. . However, in terms of rituals, by {{ill|lt=Middle Ages|Q12554|qid=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}}, the Kamo clan's character as a guardian deity had already faded, and its character as a shrine for the local people had strengthened {{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1979}}. In {{ill|lt=Horeki|Q1194397|qid=Q1194397|1=Hōreki}} 4th year ({{ill|lt=1754|Q7623|qid=Q7623|1=1754}}) "{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamashiro Famous Sites Pilgrimage|1=UNKNOWN}}" At the northern end of Shichiku, there is an old shrine called Omiya no Mori, which is called Haku no Mori, and you can see the description that {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|qid=Q234731|1=Torii}} faces west and the hall of worship faces south. Since {{ill|lt=early modern times|Q5308718|qid=Q5308718|1=Early modern period}}, our company was mainly called "Uji Shrine" or "Omiya", but {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096} In }5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|qid=Q7753|1=1872}}), the company name was changed to "Kuga Shrine" and remains the current name {{Sfn|Kuga Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. === God level === * {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} January 27, 1st year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|qid=Q23897|1=859}}), from upper 6th rank to lower rank of 5th rank (``{{ill|lt=Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|qid=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - Written as ``Kugagami.'' === Precincts === [[File:Kuga-jinja2.JPG|thumb|200px|A zelkova stump
{{Smaller|The tree is said to be 600 years old.{{Sfn|Kamigamo Shrine (Student Shrine)|2003|pp=51-52}}. }}]] In the ``{{ill|lt=Yongshu Fushi|Q18460389|qid=Q18460389|1=Yongshu Fushi}}'' written in 1686|Q7664}}, the precincts are commonly referred to as the ``Omiya Forest,'' and there are still giant {{ill|lt=zelkova|Q920344|qid=Q920344|1=Zelkova serrata}} trees on the premises that still bear traces of that past.. The temple grounds have been designated as a Kyoto City designated historic site, as the tradition of the migration of the Kamo clan has been passed down to the present day, and the environment that reveals the history of Kyoto has been preserved in good condition. The current main shrine and worship hall were built in {{ill|lt=Kanei|Q1192913|qid=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}}5 ({{ill|lt=1628|Q6840|qid=Q6840|1=1628}}){{Sfn|Kamigamo Shrine (Student Shrine)|2003|pp=51-52}}. The main shrine is in the style of Ikken-sha {{ill|lt=Nagazuzukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}, with a {{ill|lt=cypress bark roof|Q90373477|qid=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} roof, and is the same structure as other shrines of Kamigamo Shrine. The worship hall has a {{ill|lt=gabled structure|Q13308575|qid=Q13308575|1=Kirizuma-style}} gable with one beam and two beams, and the roof is also thatched with cypress bark.Furthermore, there is a legend that Kuga Shrine was called ``Omiya'' from the time of its establishment, and that the name of the area was ``Omiya'' (Omiya-go) and the street name in front of the gate was ``Omiya-dori.'' That street is {{ill|lt=Daitokuji-dori|Q11435442|qid=Q11435442|1=Daitokuji Street}}, which locals still refer to as ``Old Omiya-dori'' today. === Festival === * Spring Festival ({{ill|lt=April 1st|Q2510|qid=Q2510|1=April 1}}) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hassaku Festival|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=September 1st|Q2859|qid=Q2859|1=September 1}}) * Autumn Festival ({{ill|lt=November 1st|Q2997|qid=Q2997|1=November 1}}) * Shinko Festival ({{ill|lt=November 3rd|Q2995|qid=Q2995|1=November 3}}) - {{ill|lt=Mikoshi|Mikoshi|qid=Mikoshi|ltq=Q979940}} parades through the shrine parishioners of the four former Omiya-go school districts (Omiya, Taiho, Shichiku, and Motomachi). During the spring and autumn festivals, a ritual called ``Hikiuma-nogi'' is held in which Kamigamo Shrine's sacred horse circles the main shrine three times. === Cultural assets === === Kyoto City designated historic site === * Kuga Shrine Precincts - Designated on May 1, 1988 [https://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/bunshi/page/0000005881.html Kyoto City Designated/Registered Cultural Property - Historic
